     1→'use client';
     2→
     3→import React, { useState, useEffect } from 'react';
     4→import { Card, CardContent, CardDescription, CardHeader, CardTitle } from '@/components/ui/card';
     5→import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button';
     6→import { Badge } from '@/components/ui/badge';
     7→import { MessageSquare, Copy, TrendingUp, Calendar, Info, Star, Sparkles, User } from 'lucide-react';
     8→import { useToast } from '@/hooks/use-toast';
     9→import {
    10→  Dialog,
    11→  DialogContent,
    12→  DialogDescription,
    13→  DialogHeader,
    14→  DialogTitle,
    15→} from '@/components/ui/dialog';
    16→import { Tabs, TabsContent, TabsList, TabsTrigger } from '@/components/ui/tabs';
    17→
    18→interface MessageTemplate {
    19→  id: string;
    20→  name: string;
    21→  message_type: string;
    22→  subject?: string;
    23→  content: string;
    24→  variables: string[];
    25→  usage_count: number;
    26→  last_used_at?: string;
    27→  is_active: boolean;
    28→  created_at: string;
    29→}
    30→
    31→interface MessageExample {
    32→  id: string;
    33→  user_id: string;
    34→  client_id?: string;
    35→  campaign_id?: string;
    36→  message_type: string;
    37→  content: string;
    38→  was_ai_generated: boolean;
    39→  user_rating?: number;
    40→  was_edited: boolean;
    41→  edit_count: number;
    42→  was_sent: boolean;
    43→  created_at: string;
    44→  client_name?: string;
    45→  campaign_name?: string;
    46→}
    47→
    48→export default function TemplatesPage() {
    49→  const [templates, setTemplates] = useState<MessageTemplate[]>([]);
    50→  const [learningExamples, setLearningExamples] = useState<MessageExample[]>([]);
    51→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);
    52→  const [loadingExamples, setLoadingExamples] = useState(false);
    53→  const [selectedTemplate, setSelectedTemplate] = useState<MessageTemplate | null>(null);
    54→  const [selectedExample, setSelectedExample] = useState<MessageExample | null>(null);
    55→  const [activeTab, setActiveTab] = useState<'templates' | 'examples'>('templates');
    56→  const { toast } = useToast();
    57→
    58→  useEffect(() => {
    59→    fetchTemplates();
    60→    fetchLearningExamples();
    61→  }, []);
    62→
    63→  const fetchTemplates = async () => {
    64→    try {
    65→      const response = await fetch('/api/templates');
    66→      if (response.ok) {
    67→        const data = await response.json();
    68→        setTemplates(data);
    69→      }
    70→    } catch (error) {
    71→      console.error('Error fetching templates:', error);
    72→      toast({
    73→        title: 'Error',
    74→        description: 'Failed to load templates',
    75→        variant: 'destructive',
    76→      });
    77→    } finally {
    78→      setLoading(false);
    79→    }
    80→  };
    81→
    82→  const fetchLearningExamples = async () => {
    83→    setLoadingExamples(true);
    84→    try {
    85→      const response = await fetch('/api/templates/examples');
    86→      if (response.ok) {
    87→        const data = await response.json();
    88→        setLearningExamples(data);
    89→      }
    90→    } catch (error) {
    91→      console.error('Error fetching learning examples:', error);
    92→      toast({
    93→        title: 'Error',
    94→        description: 'Failed to load learning examples',
    95→        variant: 'destructive',
    96→      });
    97→    } finally {
    98→      setLoadingExamples(false);
    99→    }
   100→  };
   101→
   102→  const handleCopyTemplate = (template: MessageTemplate) => {
   103→    navigator.clipboard.writeText(template.content);
   104→    toast({
   105→      title: 'Copied!',
   106→      description: `${template.name} copied to clipboard`,
   107→    });
   108→  };
   109→
   110→  const getMessageTypeColor = (type: string) => {
   111→    const colors: Record<string, string> = {
   112→      initial_outreach: 'bg-blue-100 text-blue-800',
   113→      nda_request: 'bg-purple-100 text-purple-800',
   114→      kol_list_access: 'bg-green-100 text-green-800',
   115→      kol_list_delivery: 'bg-teal-100 text-teal-800',
   116→      final_kol_picks: 'bg-orange-100 text-orange-800',
   117→      post_call_followup: 'bg-yellow-100 text-yellow-800',
   118→      contract_activation: 'bg-pink-100 text-pink-800',
   119→      activation_inputs: 'bg-cyan-100 text-cyan-800',
   120→      budget_plan: 'bg-emerald-100 text-emerald-800',
   121→      outreach_update: 'bg-lime-100 text-lime-800',
   122→      finalizing_kols: 'bg-amber-100 text-amber-800',
   123→      creator_brief: 'bg-fuchsia-100 text-fuchsia-800',
   124→      final_checklist: 'bg-rose-100 text-rose-800',
   125→      activation_day: 'bg-red-100 text-red-800',
   126→      mid_campaign_update: 'bg-violet-100 text-violet-800',
   127→      initial_results: 'bg-sky-100 text-sky-800',
   128→      final_report: 'bg-indigo-100 text-indigo-800',
   129→    };
   130→    return colors[type] || 'bg-gray-100 text-gray-800';
   131→  };
   132→
   133→  const formatDate = (dateString?: string) => {
   134→    if (!dateString) return 'Never';
   135→    return new Date(dateString).toLocaleDateString('en-US', {
   136→      month: 'short',
   137→      day: 'numeric',
   138→      year: 'numeric',
   139→    });
   140→  };
   141→
   142→  const formatMessageType = (type: string) => {
   143→    const acronyms = ['nda', 'kol'];
   144→    return type.replace(/_/g, ' ').split(' ').map(word => {
   145→      if (acronyms.includes(word.toLowerCase())) {
   146→        return word.toUpperCase();
   147→      }
   148→      return word.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + word.slice(1);
   149→    }).join(' ');
   150→  };
   151→
   152→  if (loading || loadingExamples) {
   153→    return (
   154→      <div className="space-y-6">
   155→        {/* Header Skeleton */}
   156→        <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
   157→          <div>
   158→            <div className="h-8 bg-gray-200 rounded w-64 mb-2 animate-pulse"></div>
   159→            <div className="h-4 bg-gray-100 rounded w-80 animate-pulse"></div>
   160→          </div>
   161→          <div className="flex gap-2">
   162→            <div className="h-7 bg-gray-200 rounded w-24 animate-pulse"></div>
   163→            <div className="h-7 bg-gray-200 rounded w-24 animate-pulse"></div>
   164→          </div>
   165→        </div>
   166→
   167→        {/* Tabs Skeleton */}
   168→        <div className="flex gap-2 border-b">
   169→          <div className="h-10 bg-gray-200 rounded-t w-32 animate-pulse"></div>
   170→          <div className="h-10 bg-gray-100 rounded-t w-40 animate-pulse"></div>
   171→        </div>
   172→
   173→        {/* Info Card Skeleton */}
   174→        <Card className="bg-blue-50 border-blue-200">
   175→          <CardContent className="pt-6">
   176→            <div className="flex items-start gap-3">
   177→              <div className="h-5 w-5 bg-blue-200 rounded animate-pulse"></div>
   178→              <div className="flex-1 space-y-2">
   179→                <div className="h-4 bg-blue-200 rounded w-48 animate-pulse"></div>
   180→                <div className="h-3 bg-blue-100 rounded w-full animate-pulse"></div>
   181→                <div className="h-3 bg-blue-100 rounded w-3/4 animate-pulse"></div>
   182→              </div>
   183→            </div>
   184→          </CardContent>
   185→        </Card>
   186→
   187→        {/* Cards Grid Skeleton */}
   188→        <div className="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-6">
   189→          {[1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6].map((i) => (
   190→            <Card key={i} className="flex flex-col">
   191→              <CardHeader>
   192→                <div className="flex items-start justify-between mb-3">
   193→                  <div className="flex-1">
   194→                    <div className="h-5 bg-gray-200 rounded w-3/4 mb-3 animate-pulse"></div>
   195→                    <div className="h-6 bg-gray-200 rounded w-32 animate-pulse"></div>
   196→                  </div>
   197→                  <div className="h-5 w-5 bg-gray-200 rounded animate-pulse"></div>
   198→                </div>
   199→              </CardHeader>
   200→              <CardContent className="flex flex-col flex-grow">
   201→                <div className="flex-grow space-y-4">
   202→                  {/* Content Preview Skeleton */}
   203→                  <div className="bg-gray-50 p-3 rounded border border-gray-200 space-y-2">
   204→                    <div className="h-3 bg-gray-200 rounded animate-pulse"></div>
   205→                    <div className="h-3 bg-gray-200 rounded animate-pulse"></div>
   206→                    <div className="h-3 bg-gray-200 rounded w-5/6 animate-pulse"></div>
   207→                    <div className="h-3 bg-gray-200 rounded w-4/6 animate-pulse"></div>
   208→                  </div>
   209→
   210→                  {/* Variables Skeleton */}
   211→                  <div className="space-y-2">
   212→                    <div className="h-3 bg-gray-200 rounded w-20 animate-pulse"></div>
   213→                    <div className="flex gap-1">
   214→                      <div className="h-5 bg-gray-200 rounded w-16 animate-pulse"></div>
   215→                      <div className="h-5 bg-gray-200 rounded w-20 animate-pulse"></div>
   216→                    </div>
   217→                  </div>
   218→
   219→                  {/* Stats Skeleton */}
   220→                  <div className="grid grid-cols-2 gap-4 pt-4 border-t">
   221→                    <div className="space-y-1">
   222→                      <div className="h-3 bg-gray-200 rounded w-12 animate-pulse"></div>
   223→                      <div className="h-4 bg-gray-200 rounded w-8 animate-pulse"></div>
   224→                    </div>
   225→                    <div className="space-y-1">
   226→                      <div className="h-3 bg-gray-200 rounded w-16 animate-pulse"></div>
   227→                      <div className="h-3 bg-gray-200 rounded w-12 animate-pulse"></div>
   228→                    </div>
   229→                  </div>
   230→                </div>
   231→
   232→                {/* Buttons Skeleton */}
   233→                <div className="grid grid-cols-2 gap-2 pt-4 mt-auto">
   234→                  <div className="h-9 bg-gray-200 rounded animate-pulse"></div>
   235→                  <div className="h-9 bg-gray-200 rounded animate-pulse"></div>
   236→                </div>
   237→              </CardContent>
   238→            </Card>
   239→          ))}
   240→        </div>
   241→      </div>
   242→    );
   243→  }
   244→
   245→  return (
   246→    <div className="space-y-6">
   247→      {/* Header */}
   248→      <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
   249→        <div>
   250→          <h1 className="text-2xl font-bold text-gray-900">Client Message Templates</h1>
   251→          <p className="text-gray-600 mt-1">
   252→            Pre-built templates and AI learning examples
   253→          </p>
   254→        </div>
   255→        <div className="flex gap-2">
   256→          <Badge variant="secondary" className="text-sm px-3 py-1">
   257→            {templates.length} Templates
   258→          </Badge>
   259→          <Badge variant="outline" className="text-sm px-3 py-1">
   260→            {learningExamples.length} Examples
   261→          </Badge>
   262→        </div>
   263→      </div>
   264→
   265→      {/* Tabs */}
   266→      <Tabs value={activeTab} onValueChange={(value) => setActiveTab(value as 'templates' | 'examples')}>
   267→        <TabsList>
   268→          <TabsTrigger value="templates">
   269→            <MessageSquare className="h-4 w-4 mr-2" />
   270→            Templates
   271→          </TabsTrigger>
   272→          <TabsTrigger value="examples">
   273→            <Sparkles className="h-4 w-4 mr-2" />
   274→            Learning Examples
   275→          </TabsTrigger>
   276→        </TabsList>
   277→
   278→        <TabsContent value="templates" className="space-y-6">
   279→          {/* Info Card */}
   280→          <Card className="bg-blue-50 border-blue-200">
   281→            <CardContent className="pt-6">
   282→              <div className="flex items-start gap-3">
   283→                <Info className="h-5 w-5 text-blue-600 mt-0.5 flex-shrink-0" />
   284→                <div className="space-y-2">
   285→                  <p className="text-sm text-blue-900 font-medium">
   286→                    How to use templates with AI
   287→                  </p>
   288→                  <p className="text-sm text-blue-800">
   289→                    Use the AI chat to generate messages: <code className="bg-blue-100 px-2 py-0.5 rounded">Generate an initial outreach for Jdot</code>
   290→                    <br />
   291→                    The AI will use these templates and learn from messages you mark as "Sent".
   292→                  </p>
   293→                </div>
   294→              </div>
   295→            </CardContent>
   296→          </Card>
   297→
   298→      {/* Templates Grid */}
   299→      <div className="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-6">
   300→        {templates.map((template) => {
   301→          // Apply Telegram formatting for preview
   302→          const formatForTelegram = (content: string) => {
   303→            let formatted = content;
   304→            // Make section headers bold
   305→            formatted = formatted.replace(/^([A-Z][A-Za-z\s&]+):$/gm, '**$1:**');
   306→            formatted = formatted.replace(/\n([A-Z][A-Za-z\s&]+):\n/g, '\n**$1:**\n');
   307→            // Replace bullet points with emoji
   308→            formatted = formatted.replace(/^[•-]/gm, '▪️');
   309→            // Make greetings bold
   310→            formatted = formatted.replace(/^(GM|Hey team|Hi)/gm, '**$1**');
   311→            return formatted;
   312→          };
   313→
   314→          const formattedContent = formatForTelegram(template.content);
   315→
   316→          return (
   317→            <Card key={template.id} className="transition-shadow flex flex-col">
   318→              <CardHeader>
   319→                <div className="flex items-start justify-between">
   320→                  <div className="flex-1">
   321→                    <CardTitle className="text-lg font-semibold text-gray-900 mb-2">
   322→                      {template.name}
   323→                    </CardTitle>
   324→                    <Badge className={`${getMessageTypeColor(template.message_type)} pointer-events-none`}>
   325→                      {formatMessageType(template.message_type)}
   326→                    </Badge>
   327→                  </div>
   328→                  <MessageSquare className="h-5 w-5 text-gray-400" />
   329→                </div>
   330→              </CardHeader>
   331→              <CardContent className="flex flex-col flex-grow">
   332→                <div className="flex-grow space-y-4">
   333→                  {/* Content Preview with Telegram formatting */}
   334→                  <div className="text-sm text-gray-600 bg-gray-50 p-3 rounded border border-gray-200">
   335→                    <div className="line-clamp-4 whitespace-pre-wrap" style={{ wordBreak: 'break-word' }}>
   336→                      {formattedContent.split('\n').slice(0, 6).map((line, idx) => {
   337→                        // Check if line should be bold
   338→                        const isBold = line.match(/^\*\*(.+)\*\*$/);
   339→                        if (isBold) {
   340→                          return (
   341→                            <div key={idx} className="font-semibold">
   342→                              {isBold[1]}
   343→                            </div>
   344→                          );
   345→                        }
   346→                        return <div key={idx}>{line || '\u00A0'}</div>;
   347→                      }).slice(0, 4)}
   348→                      {formattedContent.split('\n').length > 4 && (
   349→                        <div className="text-gray-400 italic">...</div>
   350→                      )}
   351→                    </div>
   352→                  </div>
   353→
   354→                  {/* Variables */}
   355→                  {template.variables && template.variables.length > 0 && (
   356→                    <div>
   357→                      <p className="text-xs font-semibold text-gray-700 mb-2">Variables:</p>
   358→                      <div className="flex flex-wrap gap-1">
   359→                        {template.variables.map((variable) => (
   360→                          <Badge key={variable} variant="outline" className="text-xs">
   361→                            {variable}
   362→                          </Badge>
   363→                        ))}
   364→                      </div>
   365→                    </div>
   366→                  )}
   367→
   368→                  {/* Stats */}
   369→                  <div className="grid grid-cols-2 gap-4 pt-4 border-t">
   370→                    <div className="flex items-center gap-2 text-sm">
   371→                      <TrendingUp className="h-4 w-4 text-gray-400" />
   372→                      <div>
   373→                        <p className="text-xs text-gray-500">Used</p>
   374→                        <p className="font-semibold text-gray-900">{template.usage_count}x</p>
   375→                      </div>
   376→                    </div>
   377→                    <div className="flex items-center gap-2 text-sm">
   378→                      <Calendar className="h-4 w-4 text-gray-400" />
   379→                      <div>
   380→                        <p className="text-xs text-gray-500">Last Used</p>
   381→                        <p className="font-semibold text-gray-900 text-xs">
   382→                          {formatDate(template.last_used_at)}
   383→                        </p>
   384→                      </div>
   385→                    </div>
   386→                  </div>
   387→                </div>
   388→
   389→                {/* Actions - Always at bottom */}
   390→                <div className="grid grid-cols-2 gap-2 pt-4 mt-auto">
   391→                  <Button
   392→                    variant="outline"
   393→                    size="sm"
   394→                    onClick={() => setSelectedTemplate(template)}
   395→                    className="w-full"
   396→                  >
   397→                    View Full
   398→                  </Button>
   399→                  <Button
   400→                    variant="default"
   401→                    size="sm"
   402→                    onClick={() => handleCopyTemplate(template)}
   403→                    className="w-full"
   404→                    style={{ backgroundColor: '#3e8692' }}
   405→                  >
   406→                    <Copy className="h-4 w-4 mr-1" />
   407→                    Copy
   408→                  </Button>
   409→                </div>
   410→              </CardContent>
   411→            </Card>
   412→          );
   413→        })}
   414→      </div>
   415→        </TabsContent>
   416→
   417→        <TabsContent value="examples" className="space-y-6">
   418→          {/* Info Card */}
   419→          <Card className="bg-purple-50 border-purple-200">
   420→            <CardContent className="pt-6">
   421→              <div className="flex items-start gap-3">
   422→                <Sparkles className="h-5 w-5 text-purple-600 mt-0.5 flex-shrink-0" />
   423→                <div className="space-y-2">
   424→                  <p className="text-sm text-purple-900 font-medium">
   425→                    AI Learning Examples
   426→                  </p>
   427→                  <p className="text-sm text-purple-800">
   428→                    These are messages you've marked as "Sent" that help the AI learn your communication style and preferences.
   429→                    The AI uses these examples to generate better, more personalized messages.
   430→                  </p>
   431→                </div>
   432→              </div>
   433→            </CardContent>
   434→          </Card>
   435→
   436→          {/* Learning Examples Grid */}
   437→          {loadingExamples ? (
   438→            <div className="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-6">
   439→              {[1, 2, 3].map((i) => (
   440→                <Card key={i} className="animate-pulse">
   441→                  <CardHeader>
   442→                    <div className="h-4 bg-gray-200 rounded w-3/4 mb-2"></div>
   443→                    <div className="h-3 bg-gray-200 rounded w-1/2"></div>
   444→                  </CardHeader>
   445→                  <CardContent>
   446→                    <div className="space-y-2">
   447→                      <div className="h-3 bg-gray-200 rounded"></div>
   448→                      <div className="h-3 bg-gray-200 rounded"></div>
   449→                      <div className="h-3 bg-gray-200 rounded w-5/6"></div>
   450→                    </div>
   451→                  </CardContent>
   452→                </Card>
   453→              ))}
   454→            </div>
   455→          ) : learningExamples.length === 0 ? (
   456→            <Card>
   457→              <CardContent className="pt-6 text-center py-12">
   458→                <Sparkles className="h-12 w-12 text-gray-400 mx-auto mb-4" />
   459→                <p className="text-gray-600">No learning examples yet.</p>
   460→                <p className="text-sm text-gray-500 mt-2">
   461→                  Mark generated messages as "Sent" to create learning examples for the AI.
   462→                </p>
   463→              </CardContent>
   464→            </Card>
   465→          ) : (
   466→            <div className="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-6">
   467→              {learningExamples.map((example) => (
   468→                <Card key={example.id} className="transition-shadow flex flex-col">
   469→                  <CardHeader>
   470→                    <div className="flex items-start justify-between">
   471→                      <div className="flex-1">
   472→                        <CardTitle className="text-lg font-semibold text-gray-900 mb-2">
   473→                          {example.client_name || 'Unknown Client'}
   474→                        </CardTitle>
   475→                        <div className="flex flex-wrap gap-2">
   476→                          <Badge className={`${getMessageTypeColor(example.message_type)} pointer-events-none`}>
   477→                            {formatMessageType(example.message_type)}
   478→                          </Badge>
   479→                          {example.was_ai_generated ? (
   480→                            <Badge variant="secondary" className="bg-purple-100 text-purple-800 pointer-events-none">
   481→                              <Sparkles className="h-3 w-3 mr-1" />
   482→                              AI Generated
   483→                            </Badge>
   484→                          ) : (
   485→                            <Badge variant="secondary" className="bg-blue-100 text-blue-800 pointer-events-none">
   486→                              <User className="h-3 w-3 mr-1" />
   487→                              User Provided
   488→                            </Badge>
   489→                          )}
   490→                        </div>
   491→                      </div>
   492→                    </div>
   493→                  </CardHeader>
   494→                  <CardContent className="flex flex-col flex-grow">
   495→                    <div className="flex-grow space-y-4">
   496→                      {/* Content Preview */}
   497→                      <div className="text-sm text-gray-600 bg-gray-50 p-3 rounded border border-gray-200">
   498→                        <div className="line-clamp-4 whitespace-pre-wrap" style={{ wordBreak: 'break-word' }}>
   499→                          {example.content.split('\n').slice(0, 4).map((line, idx) => (
   500→                            <div key={idx}>{line || '\u00A0'}</div>
   501→                          ))}
   502→                          {example.content.split('\n').length > 4 && (
   503→                            <div className="text-gray-400 italic">...</div>
   504→                          )}
   505→                        </div>
   506→                      </div>
   507→
   508→                      {/* Campaign Info */}
   509→                      {example.campaign_name && (
   510→                        <div className="text-xs text-gray-500">
   511→                          Campaign: <span className="font-semibold">{example.campaign_name}</span>
   512→                        </div>
   513→                      )}
   514→
   515→                      {/* Stats */}
   516→                      <div className="grid grid-cols-2 gap-4 pt-4 border-t">
   517→                        <div className="flex items-center gap-2 text-sm">
   518→                          <Star className="h-4 w-4 text-yellow-400" />
   519→                          <div>
   520→                            <p className="text-xs text-gray-500">Rating</p>
   521→                            <p className="font-semibold text-gray-900">
   522→                              {example.user_rating ? `${example.user_rating}/5` : 'N/A'}
   523→                            </p>
   524→                          </div>
   525→                        </div>
   526→                        <div className="flex items-center gap-2 text-sm">
   527→                          <Calendar className="h-4 w-4 text-gray-400" />
   528→                          <div>
   529→                            <p className="text-xs text-gray-500">Created</p>
   530→                            <p className="font-semibold text-gray-900 text-xs">
   531→                              {formatDate(example.created_at)}
   532→                            </p>
   533→                          </div>
   534→                        </div>
   535→                      </div>
   536→
   537→                      {/* Edit Count */}
   538→                      {example.was_edited && (
   539→                        <div className="text-xs text-gray-500">
   540→                          Edited {example.edit_count} time{example.edit_count !== 1 ? 's' : ''}
   541→                        </div>
   542→                      )}
   543→                    </div>
   544→
   545→                    {/* Actions - Always at bottom */}
   546→                    <div className="grid grid-cols-2 gap-2 pt-4 mt-auto">
   547→                      <Button
   548→                        variant="outline"
   549→                        size="sm"
   550→                        onClick={() => setSelectedExample(example)}
   551→                        className="w-full"
   552→                      >
   553→                        View Full
   554→                      </Button>
   555→                      <Button
   556→                        variant="default"
   557→                        size="sm"
   558→                        onClick={() => {
   559→                          navigator.clipboard.writeText(example.content);
   560→                          toast({
   561→                            title: 'Copied!',
   562→                            description: 'Message copied to clipboard',
   563→                          });
   564→                        }}
   565→                        className="w-full"
   566→                        style={{ backgroundColor: '#3e8692' }}
   567→                      >
   568→                        <Copy className="h-4 w-4 mr-1" />
   569→                        Copy
   570→                      </Button>
   571→                    </div>
   572→                  </CardContent>
   573→                </Card>
   574→              ))}
   575→            </div>
   576→          )}
   577→        </TabsContent>
   578→      </Tabs>
   579→
   580→      {/* Preview Dialog for Templates */}
   581→      <Dialog open={!!selectedTemplate} onOpenChange={() => setSelectedTemplate(null)}>
   582→        <DialogContent className="max-w-2xl max-h-[80vh] overflow-y-auto">
   583→          <DialogHeader>
   584→            <DialogTitle className="text-xl">{selectedTemplate?.name}</DialogTitle>
   585→            <DialogDescription>
   586→              <Badge className={getMessageTypeColor(selectedTemplate?.message_type || '')}>
   587→                {formatMessageType(selectedTemplate?.message_type || '')}
   588→              </Badge>
   589→            </DialogDescription>
   590→          </DialogHeader>
   591→
   592→          {selectedTemplate && (
   593→            <div className="space-y-4">
   594→              {/* Variables */}
   595→              {selectedTemplate.variables && selectedTemplate.variables.length > 0 && (
   596→                <div>
   597→                  <h3 className="text-sm font-semibold text-gray-900 mb-2">Available Variables</h3>
   598→                  <div className="flex flex-wrap gap-2">
   599→                    {selectedTemplate.variables.map((variable) => (
   600→                      <Badge key={variable} variant="secondary" className="font-mono">
   601→                        [{variable}]
   602→                      </Badge>
   603→                    ))}
   604→                  </div>
   605→                  <p className="text-xs text-gray-500 mt-2">
   606→                    These variables are automatically filled when generating messages via AI
   607→                  </p>
   608→                </div>
   609→              )}
   610→
   611→              {/* Full Content */}
   612→              <div>
   613→                <h3 className="text-sm font-semibold text-gray-900 mb-2">Template Content</h3>
   614→                <pre className="text-sm bg-gray-50 p-4 rounded border border-gray-200 whitespace-pre-wrap font-sans">
   615→                  {selectedTemplate.content}
   616→                </pre>
   617→              </div>
   618→
   619→              {/* Stats */}
   620→              <div className="grid grid-cols-3 gap-4 pt-4 border-t">
   621→                <div>
   622→                  <p className="text-xs text-gray-500">Usage Count</p>
   623→                  <p className="text-lg font-semibold text-gray-900">{selectedTemplate.usage_count}</p>
   624→                </div>
   625→                <div>
   626→                  <p className="text-xs text-gray-500">Last Used</p>
   627→                  <p className="text-sm font-semibold text-gray-900">
   628→                    {formatDate(selectedTemplate.last_used_at)}
   629→                  </p>
   630→                </div>
   631→                <div>
   632→                  <p className="text-xs text-gray-500">Created</p>
   633→                  <p className="text-sm font-semibold text-gray-900">
   634→                    {formatDate(selectedTemplate.created_at)}
   635→                  </p>
   636→                </div>
   637→              </div>
   638→
   639→              {/* Actions */}
   640→              <div className="flex gap-2 pt-4">
   641→                <Button
   642→                  onClick={() => handleCopyTemplate(selectedTemplate)}
   643→                  className="flex-1"
   644→                  style={{ backgroundColor: '#3e8692' }}
   645→                >
   646→                  <Copy className="h-4 w-4 mr-2" />
   647→                  Copy Template
   648→                </Button>
   649→                <Button
   650→                  variant="outline"
   651→                  onClick={() => setSelectedTemplate(null)}
   652→                  className="flex-1"
   653→                >
   654→                  Close
   655→                </Button>
   656→              </div>
   657→            </div>
   658→          )}
   659→        </DialogContent>
   660→      </Dialog>
   661→
   662→      {/* Preview Dialog for Learning Examples */}
   663→      <Dialog open={!!selectedExample} onOpenChange={() => setSelectedExample(null)}>
   664→        <DialogContent className="max-w-2xl max-h-[80vh] overflow-y-auto">
   665→          <DialogHeader>
   666→            <DialogTitle className="text-xl">{selectedExample?.client_name || 'Unknown Client'}</DialogTitle>
   667→            <DialogDescription>
   668→              <div className="flex flex-wrap gap-2 mt-2">
   669→                <Badge className={getMessageTypeColor(selectedExample?.message_type || '')}>
   670→                  {formatMessageType(selectedExample?.message_type || '')}
   671→                </Badge>
   672→                {selectedExample?.was_ai_generated ? (
   673→                  <Badge variant="secondary" className="bg-purple-100 text-purple-800">
   674→                    <Sparkles className="h-3 w-3 mr-1" />
   675→                    AI Generated
   676→                  </Badge>
   677→                ) : (
   678→                  <Badge variant="secondary" className="bg-blue-100 text-blue-800">
   679→                    <User className="h-3 w-3 mr-1" />
   680→                    User Provided
   681→                  </Badge>
   682→                )}
   683→              </div>
   684→            </DialogDescription>
   685→          </DialogHeader>
   686→
   687→          {selectedExample && (
   688→            <div className="space-y-4">
   689→              {/* Full Content */}
   690→              <div>
   691→                <h3 className="text-sm font-semibold text-gray-900 mb-2">Message Content</h3>
   692→                <pre className="text-sm bg-gray-50 p-4 rounded border border-gray-200 whitespace-pre-wrap font-sans">
   693→                  {selectedExample.content}
   694→                </pre>
   695→              </div>
   696→
   697→              {/* Campaign Info */}
   698→              {selectedExample.campaign_name && (
   699→                <div>
   700→                  <h3 className="text-sm font-semibold text-gray-900 mb-2">Campaign</h3>
   701→                  <p className="text-sm text-gray-600">{selectedExample.campaign_name}</p>
   702→                </div>
   703→              )}
   704→
   705→              {/* Stats */}
   706→              <div className="grid grid-cols-3 gap-4 pt-4 border-t">
   707→                <div>
   708→                  <p className="text-xs text-gray-500">Rating</p>

   718→                                : 'text-gray-300'
   719→                            }`}
   720→                          />
   721→                        ))}
   722→                        <span className="ml-1 text-sm font-semibold">{selectedExample.user_rating}/5</span>
   723→                      </>
   724→                    ) : (
   725→                      <span className="text-sm text-gray-400">No rating</span>
   726→                    )}
   727→                  </div>
   728→                </div>
   729→                <div>
   730→                  <p className="text-xs text-gray-500">Edit Count</p>
   731→                  <p className="text-sm font-semibold text-gray-900 mt-1">
   732→                    {selectedExample.edit_count} edit{selectedExample.edit_count !== 1 ? 's' : ''}
   733→                  </p>
   734→                </div>
   735→                <div>
   736→                  <p className="text-xs text-gray-500">Created</p>
   737→                  <p className="text-sm font-semibold text-gray-900 mt-1">
   738→                    {formatDate(selectedExample.created_at)}
   739→                  </p>
   740→                </div>
   741→              </div>
   742→
   743→              {/* Actions */}
   744→              <div className="flex gap-2 pt-4">
   745→                <Button
   746→                  onClick={() => {
   747→                    navigator.clipboard.writeText(selectedExample.content);
   748→                    toast({
   749→                      title: 'Copied!',
   750→                      description: 'Message copied to clipboard',
   751→                    });
   752→                  }}
   753→                  className="flex-1"
   754→                  style={{ backgroundColor: '#3e8692' }}
   755→                >
   756→                  <Copy className="h-4 w-4 mr-2" />
   757→                  Copy Message

   763→                >
   764→                  Close
   765→                </Button>
   766→              </div>
   767→            </div>
   768→          )}

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./app/api/templates/route.ts`

```text
     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server';
     2→import { createServerClient } from '@supabase/ssr';
     3→import { cookies } from 'next/headers';
     4→
     5→export async function GET(request: NextRequest) {
     6→  try {
     7→    const cookieStore = await cookies();
     8→    const supabase = createServerClient(
     9→      process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!,
    10→      process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY!,
    11→      {
    12→        cookies: {
    13→          get(name: string) {
    14→            return cookieStore.get(name)?.value;
    15→          },
    16→        },
    17→      }
    18→    );
    19→
    20→    const {
    21→      data: { user },
    22→    } = await supabase.auth.getUser();
    23→
    24→    if (!user) {
    25→      return NextResponse.json({ error: 'Unauthorized' }, { status: 401 });
    26→    }
    27→
    28→    // Fetch all active message templates
    29→    const { data: templates, error } = await supabase
    30→      .from('message_templates')
    31→      .select('*')
    32→      .eq('is_active', true)
    33→      .order('usage_count', { ascending: false });
    34→
    35→    if (error) {
    36→      console.error('Error fetching templates:', error);
    37→      return NextResponse.json(
    38→        { error: 'Failed to fetch templates' },
    39→        { status: 500 }
    40→      );
    41→    }
    42→
    43→    return NextResponse.json(templates || []);
    44→  } catch (error) {
    45→    console.error('Error in templates API:', error);
    46→    return NextResponse.json(
    47→      { error: 'Internal server error' },
    48→      { status: 500 }
    49→    );
    50→  }
    51→}
    52→
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./app/api/templates/examples/route.ts`

```text
     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server';
     2→import { createServerClient } from '@supabase/ssr';
     3→import { cookies } from 'next/headers';
     4→
     5→export async function GET(request: NextRequest) {
     6→  try {
     7→    const cookieStore = await cookies();
     8→    const supabase = createServerClient(
     9→      process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!,
    10→      process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY!,
    11→      {
    12→        cookies: {
    13→          get(name: string) {
    14→            return cookieStore.get(name)?.value;
    15→          },
    16→        },
    17→      }
    18→    );
    19→
    20→    const {
    21→      data: { user },
    22→    } = await supabase.auth.getUser();
    23→
    24→    if (!user) {
    25→      return NextResponse.json({ error: 'Unauthorized' }, { status: 401 });
    26→    }
    27→
    28→    // Fetch all message examples that are marked as sent (used for learning)
    29→    const { data: examples, error } = await supabase
    30→      .from('client_message_examples')
    31→      .select(`
    32→        *,
    33→        client:clients(name),
    34→        campaign:campaigns(name)
    35→      `)
    36→      .eq('was_sent', true)
    37→      .order('user_rating', { ascending: false, nullsFirst: false })
    38→      .order('created_at', { ascending: false });
    39→
    40→    if (error) {
    41→      console.error('Error fetching learning examples:', error);
    42→      return NextResponse.json(
    43→        { error: 'Failed to fetch learning examples' },
    44→        { status: 500 }
    45→      );
    46→    }
    47→
    48→    // Transform the data to flatten client and campaign names
    49→    const transformedExamples = examples?.map(example => ({
    50→      ...example,
    51→      client_name: example.client?.name,
    52→      campaign_name: example.campaign?.name,
    53→    })) || [];
    54→
    55→    return NextResponse.json(transformedExamples);
    56→  } catch (error) {
    57→    console.error('Error in learning examples API:', error);
    58→    return NextResponse.json(
    59→      { error: 'Internal server error' },
    60→      { status: 500 }
    61→    );
    62→  }
    63→}
    64→
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-02-26 14:32:39Z)**_

Perfect! I now have a comprehensive understanding of the existing template system. Let me compile the findings:

## Summary of Template System in KOL Campaign Manager

### 1. **Database Migrations (Message Templates)**

**Files:**
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/sql/migrations/010_add_message_templates_and_learning.sql`
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/sql/migrations/011_update_all_message_templates.sql`

**Key Tables Created:**
- `message_templates` - Stores reusable message templates with:
  - name, message_type, subject, content
  - variables (JSONB array for placeholders like `[CLIENT_NAME]`)
  - usage_count, last_used_at, is_active
  - Row-level security policies for access control

- `client_message_examples` - Learning database that tracks:
  - user_id, client_id, campaign_id
  - message_type, subject, content
  - was_ai_generated, original_ai_content
  - user_rating, was_edited, edit_count, was_sent
  - embedding (vector for similarity search)

- `ai_message_feedback` - Detailed feedback tracking:
  - feedback_type ('edit', 'rating', 'sent', 'discarded')
  - before/after content comparison
  - helpful_score

- `template_usage_analytics` - Performance tracking:
  - generated_count, sent_count, edited_count, average_rating
  - period_start/end for time-based analytics

**Helper Functions:**
- `increment_template_usage()` - Updates template usage metrics
- `search_similar_messages()` - Vector similarity search for learning examples

**Pre-loaded Templates (17 total):**
Initial Outreach, NDA Request, KOL List Access, KOL List Delivery, Final KOL Picks, Post-Call Follow-Up, Contract & Activation, Activation Inputs, Budget Plan, Outreach Progress, Finalizing KOLs, Creator Brief, Final Checklist, Activation Day, Mid-Campaign Update, Initial Results, Final Report

---

### 2. **Service Layer - MessageTemplateService**

**File:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/messageTemplateService.ts`

**Key Methods:**
- `getTemplates()` - Fetches all active templates
- `getTemplateByType(messageType)` - Gets specific template by message type
- `fillTemplate(template, variables)` - Replaces `[VARIABLE]` placeholders
- `formatForTelegram(content)` - Applies Telegram markdown formatting
- `extractVariables(content)` - Parses variable placeholders from content
- `generateEmbedding(text)` - Creates vector embeddings using OpenAI
- `findSimilarExamples(messageType, query)` - Vector similarity search
- `generateMessage(options)` - Main function that:
  - Gets template by type
  - Fills variables
  - Finds similar examples for context
  - Uses GPT-4o to enhance/improve message
  - Formats for Telegram
  - Increments usage count
- `saveMessageExample(userId, messageType, content)` - Saves messages for learning with embedding
- `trackFeedback(messageExampleId, feedbackType)` - Records user feedback
- `getMessageStats(userId, messageType)` - Aggregates message statistics

**Interfaces:**
- `MessageTemplate` - Template structure
- `MessageExample` - Learning example structure
- `GenerateMessageOptions` - Parameters for message generation

---

### 3. **Campaign Templates Service**

**File:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/campaignTemplateService.ts`

**Note:** This is for **campaign templates** (project setup templates), NOT message templates. Uses different DB tables:
- `campaign_templates`
- `campaign_template_budget_allocations`

This is separate from the message template system and handles campaign workflow templates.

---

### 4. **Frontend - Templates Page**

**File:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/templates/page.tsx`

**Features:**
- Two tabs: "Templates" and "Learning Examples"
- Template cards showing:
  - Template name and type (with color-coded badges)
  - Content preview (4 lines with Telegram formatting)
  - Variables list
  - Usage count and last used date
  - Copy and View Full buttons
- Learning examples cards showing:
  - Client/campaign names
  - AI Generated or User Provided indicator
  - Content preview
  - User rating (1-5 stars)
  - Edit count and creation date
- Dialogs for full preview of templates and examples
- Color-coded message types (18 different colors for different message stages)

---

### 5. **API Endpoints**

**Template Endpoint:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/api/templates/route.ts`
- GET - Returns all active message templates sorted by usage count
- Requires authentication

**Learning Examples Endpoint:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/api/templates/examples/route.ts`
- GET - Returns all message examples marked as sent
- Joins with clients and campaigns tables to get names
- Sorted by user rating (highest first), then creation date
- Requires authentication

---

## Key Architecture Insights for Sales Pipeline Templates

The existing system is **message-focused** with these patterns you can follow:

1. **Naming Convention:** Uses `message_type` field to categorize templates (snake_case)
2. **Variables:** Uses `[PLACEHOLDER]` format in content, stored as JSONB array
3. **Learning:** Saves all generated messages as examples with embeddings for AI context
4. **Vector Search:** Uses pgvector + OpenAI embeddings for similarity matching
5. **Feedback Loop:** Tracks edits, ratings, and whether messages were sent
6. **UI Pattern:** Grid layout with card previews, color-coded by type, copy/view actions

For **sales pipeline stage templates**, you could create:
- A parallel `pipeline_stage_templates` table (similar to `message_templates`)
- Or extend the message templates with a `category` field to support multiple template types
- Use the same vector embedding approach for similar example lookup

---

## 1. `getNextAction` — Stages and Bump Labels

**File:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/crm/sales-pipeline/page.tsx` (lines 854–1055)

The function returns an object with `{ label, hint, priority, actionType, targetStage, isActionable, sortScore, alternatives }`.

### Return types used

```ts
type ActionPriority = 'urgent' | 'high' | 'medium' | 'low' | 'wait';
type ActionExecutionType = 'bump' | 'stage_change' | 'open_detail' | 'none';
```

### Stage-by-stage labels

| Stage | Condition | Primary Label | actionType |
|---|---|---|---|
| (any active, past meeting) | `hoursSinceMeeting` 0–168, not yet logged | `"Log Meeting Outcome"` | `open_detail` |
| (post-discovery) | no future meeting, no proposal yet | `"Send Proposal"` | `stage_change → proposal_sent` |
| (post-discovery) | no future meeting | `"Book Next Meeting!"` | `open_detail` |
| `v2_contract` | always | `"Chase Signature"` | `open_detail` |
| `proposal_call` | always | `"To Contract"` | `stage_change → v2_contract` |
| **`cold_dm`** | `bump_number >= 4` | `"Review → Orbit"` | `stage_change → orbit` |
| **`cold_dm`** | `bump_number === 0 && !last_bump_date` | **`"Send First DM"`** | `bump` |
| **`cold_dm`** | `last_bump_date` exists, `daysSince >= 3` | **`"Bump #${opp.bump_number + 1}"`** | `bump` |
| **`cold_dm`** | `last_bump_date` exists, `daysSince < 3` | `"Wait ${daysLeft}d"` | `none` |
| **`cold_dm`** | fallback (no last_bump_date, bump_number > 0) | **`"Bump #${opp.bump_number + 1}"`** | `bump` |
| `warm` | `warm_sub_state === 'interested'` | `"Get TG Handle"` | `open_detail` |
| `warm` | otherwise | `"Follow Up"` | `open_detail` |
| `tg_intro` | `daysSinceContact >= 7` | `"Re-engage or Orbit"` | `open_detail` |
| `tg_intro` | otherwise | `"Booked!"` | `stage_change → booked` |
| `booked` | no `next_meeting_at` | `"Schedule Meeting"` | `open_detail` |
| `booked` | meeting in future | `"Prep for Meeting"` | `open_detail` |
| `booked` | meeting in past | `"Discovery Done"` | `stage_change → discovery_done` |
| `discovery_done` | no `proposal_sent_at` | `"Send Proposal"` | `stage_change → proposal_sent` |
| `discovery_done` | proposal already sent | `"Update Stage"` | `stage_change → proposal_sent` |
| `proposal_sent` | always | `"Follow Up Proposal"` | `open_detail` |
| `orbit` | `daysSinceUpdated > 90` | `"Resurrect"` | `open_detail` |
| `orbit` | otherwise | `"In Orbit"` | `none` |
| `nurture` | `daysSinceNurtureContact >= 30` | `"Check In"` | `open_detail` |

**Key bump label pattern:** `"Send First DM"` is only shown when `bump_number === 0 && !last_bump_date`. Every subsequent bump shows `"Bump #${opp.bump_number + 1}"` (i.e., "Bump #1", "Bump #2", "Bump #3" until `bump_number >= 4` triggers orbit).

---

## 2. `bump_count` / `bump_number` Usage

There is no field named `bump_count` anywhere in this codebase. The field is called **`bump_number`** throughout.

**On the `SalesPipelineOpportunity` interface** (line 104):
```ts
bump_number: number;
last_bump_date: string | null;
```

**In `getNextAction`** (page.tsx):
- `opp.bump_number >= 4` → triggers "Review → Orbit"
- `opp.bump_number === 0 && !opp.last_bump_date` → "Send First DM"
- `Bump #${opp.bump_number + 1}` → shows the next bump number to send

**In `getColdDmsPaginated` filters** (service, line 541–547):
```ts
if (filters.bumpRange === 'none')      query = query.eq('bump_number', 0);
else if (filters.bumpRange === '1-2')  query = query.gte('bump_number', 1).lte('bump_number', 2);
else if (filters.bumpRange === '3+')   query = query.gte('bump_number', 3);
```

---

## 3. `SalesPipelineService` — All Methods and Tables

**File:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/salesPipelineService.ts`

### Supabase Tables Referenced

| Table | Operations |
|---|---|
| `crm_opportunities` | SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE (primary table for all ops) |
| `crm_affiliates` | JOIN via `affiliate:crm_affiliates(*)` |
| `clients` | JOIN via `client:clients!crm_opportunities_client_id_fkey(id, name)` |
| `crm_activities` | SELECT, INSERT |

### RPC Functions Called

| RPC | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `calculate_temperature_score(opp_id)` | Recalculate temperature for one opp |
| `recalculate_all_temperature_scores()` | Recalculate all temperatures |

### All Public Static Methods

| Method | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `getAll()` | `→ SalesPipelineOpportunity[]` | Fetch all opps in `ALL_V2_STAGES`, ordered by position then created_at |
| `create(data)` | `CreateSalesPipelineOpportunityData → SalesPipelineOpportunity` | Insert new opp, dedup check on name+tg_handle, records stage history, recalcs temperature |
| `update(id, updates)` | `Partial<...> → SalesPipelineOpportunity` | Update opp, auto-sets timestamps on stage transitions, records stage history, recalcs temperature |
| `delete(id)` | `→ void` | Hard delete from `crm_opportunities` |
| `recordBump(id)` | `→ SalesPipelineOpportunity` | Increments `bump_number`, sets `last_bump_date` + `last_contacted_at`, creates a `bump` activity |
| `reduceBump(id)` | `→ SalesPipelineOpportunity` | Decrements `bump_number` (floor 0), no date changes |
| `getActivities(opportunityId)` | `→ CRMActivity[]` | Fetch all activities for an opp, desc by created_at |
| `createActivity(data)` | `CreateActivityData → CRMActivity` | Insert into `crm_activities`, recalcs temperature |
| `getBAMFAMViolations()` | `→ SalesPipelineOpportunity[]` | Post-discovery opps with null or past `next_meeting_at` |
| `getAvailableStages(dmAccount)` | `→ SalesPipelineStage[]` | Returns PATH_A (closer) or PATH_B (sdr/other) stages |
| `getMetrics()` | `→ { totalCount, bucketA, bucketB, bucketC, activeValue, bamfamViolations }` | Aggregate counts from `crm_opportunities` |
| `updatePositions(positions)` | `→ void` | Bulk update `position` (and optionally `stage`) for drag-and-drop |
| `getColdDmsPaginated(page, pageSize, filters)` | `→ { data, count }` | Paginated cold_dm query with dm_account, bucket, bumpRange, search, sort filters |
| `recalcTemperature(id)` | `→ number | null` | Call RPC for one opp |
| `recalcAllTemperatures()` | `→ number | null` | Call RPC for all opps |
| `bulkRecordBump(ids)` | `→ void` | Parallel `recordBump` across multiple ids |
| `bulkUpdateStage(ids, stage)` | `→ void` | Parallel stage update for multiple ids |
| `bulkDelete(ids)` | `→ void` | Parallel delete for multiple ids |

---

## 4. `message_templates` Table Schema

**File:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/sql/migrations/010_add_message_templates_and_learning.sql`

```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS message_templates (
  id           UUID      PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  name         TEXT      NOT NULL,
  message_type TEXT      NOT NULL,  -- e.g. 'proposal', 'nda_request', 'kol_list_delivery'
  subject      TEXT,                -- nullable (used for email subject lines)
  content      TEXT      NOT NULL,
  variables    JSONB     DEFAULT '[]'::jsonb,  -- e.g. ["CLIENT_NAME", "PROJECT_NAME"]
  usage_count  INTEGER   DEFAULT 0,
  last_used_at TIMESTAMP,
  is_active    BOOLEAN   DEFAULT true,
  created_by   UUID      REFERENCES users(id),
  created_at   TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at   TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);
```

Indexes: `idx_message_templates_type` on `message_type`, `idx_message_templates_active` on `is_active`.

RLS: All authenticated users can SELECT where `is_active = true`; only `admin` role users can INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE.

### Seeded `message_type` values (from initial INSERT block)

| `message_type` | `name` | Variables |
|---|---|---|
| `initial_outreach` | Initial Outreach / Proposal | `CLIENT_NAME`, `PROJECT_NAME`, `TGE_LAUNCH` |
| `nda_request` | NDA Request | (none) |
| `kol_list_access` | KOL List Access Coordination | (none) |
| `kol_list_delivery` | KOL List Delivery | `CLIENT_HANDLE`, `EMAIL_ADDRESS` |
| `final_kol_picks` | Final KOL Picks & Strategy | `CLIENT_HANDLE` |
| `post_call_followup` | Post-Call Follow-Up | `CLIENT_HANDLE` |
| `contract_activation` | Contract & Activation Details | `CLIENT_HANDLE`, `EMAIL_ADDRESS` |
| `activation_day` | Activation Day Update | (none) |
| `final_report` | Final Campaign Report | `TGE_LAUNCH`, `CAMPAIGN_REPORT_LINK` |

The migration also creates three companion tables: `client_message_examples` (stores actual sent messages with vector embeddings for AI learning), `ai_message_feedback` (detailed edit/rating feedback), and `template_usage_analytics` (aggregated performance by time period).

   854→  // Actions Tab: getNextAction helper
   855→  // ============================================
   856→
   857→  type ActionPriority = 'urgent' | 'high' | 'medium' | 'low' | 'wait';
   858→  type ActionExecutionType = 'bump' | 'stage_change' | 'open_detail' | 'none';
   859→
   860→  type ActionAlternative = { label: string; actionType: ActionExecutionType; targetStage?: SalesPipelineStage; variant: 'default' | 'warn' | 'danger'; quick?: boolean };
   861→
   862→  const getNextAction = (opp: SalesPipelineOpportunity): {
   863→    label: string;
   864→    hint: string;
   865→    priority: ActionPriority;
   866→    actionType: ActionExecutionType;
   867→    targetStage?: SalesPipelineStage;
   868→    isActionable: boolean;
   869→    sortScore: number;
   870→    alternatives: ActionAlternative[];
   871→  } => {
   872→    const postDiscovery: SalesPipelineStage[] = ['discovery_done', 'proposal_sent', 'proposal_call', 'v2_contract'];
   873→    const terminalStages = ['v2_closed_won', 'v2_closed_lost'];
   874→
   875→    // Past meeting check — if there's a meeting in the past and deal is still active, prompt to log outcome
   876→    // Skip if the opp was already updated after the meeting (rep likely already took action)
   877→    if (!terminalStages.includes(opp.stage) && opp.stage !== 'orbit' && opp.next_meeting_at) {
   878→      const meetingTime = new Date(opp.next_meeting_at);
   879→      const now = new Date();
   880→      const hoursSinceMeeting = (now.getTime() - meetingTime.getTime()) / (1000 * 60 * 60);
   881→      const lastContactAfterMeeting = opp.last_contacted_at && new Date(opp.last_contacted_at) > meetingTime;
   882→      // Meeting was in the past (within 7 days), not yet logged
   883→      if (hoursSinceMeeting > 0 && hoursSinceMeeting <= 168 && !lastContactAfterMeeting) {
   884→        // For booked stage, the existing "Discovery Done" action handles this
   885→        if (opp.stage !== 'booked') {
   886→          return { label: 'Log Meeting Outcome', hint: `Meeting was ${hoursSinceMeeting < 24 ? 'today' : `${Math.floor(hoursSinceMeeting / 24)}d ago`} — record what happened`, priority: 'urgent', actionType: 'open_detail', isActionable: true, sortScore: 0, alternatives: [
   887→            { label: 'Reschedule', actionType: 'open_detail', variant: 'default' },
   888→            { label: 'No Show', actionType: 'open_detail', variant: 'warn' },
   889→          ]};
   890→        }
   891→      }
   892→    }
   893→
   894→    // BAMFAM violation — any post-discovery stage with no future meeting
   895→    // For discovery_done without proposal, show both BAMFAM + Send Proposal context
   896→    if (postDiscovery.includes(opp.stage as SalesPipelineStage)) {
   897→      const hasFutureMeeting = opp.next_meeting_at && new Date(opp.next_meeting_at) > new Date();
   898→      if (!hasFutureMeeting) {
   899→        // Fix #4: discovery_done without proposal — show Send Proposal as primary, BAMFAM as hint
   900→        if (opp.stage === 'discovery_done' && !opp.proposal_sent_at) {
   901→          return { label: 'Send Proposal', hint: 'Draft & send proposal. Also book a follow-up meeting (BAMFAM).', priority: 'urgent', actionType: 'stage_change', targetStage: 'proposal_sent', isActionable: true, sortScore: 1, alternatives: [
   902→            { label: 'Book Meeting', actionType: 'open_detail', variant: 'default' },
   903→            { label: 'Lost', actionType: 'stage_change', targetStage: 'v2_closed_lost', variant: 'danger' },
   904→          ]};
   905→        }
   906→        return { label: 'Book Next Meeting!', hint: 'No future meeting set — DM to schedule', priority: 'urgent', actionType: 'open_detail', isActionable: true, sortScore: 1, alternatives: [
   907→          { label: 'Orbit', actionType: 'stage_change', targetStage: 'orbit', variant: 'warn' },
   908→          { label: 'Lost', actionType: 'stage_change', targetStage: 'v2_closed_lost', variant: 'danger' },
   909→        ]};
   910→      }
   911→    }
   912→
   913→    switch (opp.stage) {
   914→      // Fix #2: v2_contract — "Chase Signature" as primary (open_detail), "Signed!" as quick alt
   915→      case 'v2_contract':
   916→        return { label: 'Chase Signature', hint: 'Follow up on the contract — get it signed', priority: 'urgent', actionType: 'open_detail', isActionable: true, sortScore: 2, alternatives: [
   917→          { label: 'Signed!', actionType: 'stage_change', targetStage: 'v2_closed_won', variant: 'default', quick: true },
   918→          { label: 'Lost', actionType: 'stage_change', targetStage: 'v2_closed_lost', variant: 'danger' },
   919→        ]};
   920→
   921→      case 'proposal_call':
   922→        return { label: 'To Contract', hint: 'Handle objections → move to contract', priority: 'urgent', actionType: 'stage_change', targetStage: 'v2_contract', isActionable: true, sortScore: 3, alternatives: [
   923→          { label: 'Follow Up', actionType: 'open_detail', variant: 'default' },
   924→          { label: 'Orbit', actionType: 'stage_change', targetStage: 'orbit', variant: 'warn' },
   925→          { label: 'Lost', actionType: 'stage_change', targetStage: 'v2_closed_lost', variant: 'danger' },
   926→        ]};
   927→
   928→      case 'cold_dm': {
   929→        if (opp.bump_number >= 4) {
   930→          return { label: 'Review → Orbit', hint: '4 bumps sent, no reply — shelve or orbit', priority: 'medium', actionType: 'stage_change', targetStage: 'orbit', isActionable: true, sortScore: 50, alternatives: [
   931→            { label: 'Replied!', actionType: 'stage_change', targetStage: 'warm', variant: 'default', quick: true },
   932→            { label: 'Keep Bumping', actionType: 'open_detail', variant: 'default' },
   933→          ]};
   934→        }
   935→        const bumpAlts: ActionAlternative[] = [
   936→          { label: 'Replied!', actionType: 'stage_change', targetStage: 'warm', variant: 'default', quick: true },
   937→          { label: 'Orbit', actionType: 'stage_change', targetStage: 'orbit', variant: 'warn' },
   938→        ];
   939→        // Fix #6: bump_number=0 but has last_bump_date (was reduced) — show "Bump" not "Send First DM"
   940→        if (opp.bump_number === 0 && !opp.last_bump_date) {
   941→          return { label: 'Send First DM', hint: 'Send intro DM on IG/X', priority: 'high', actionType: 'bump', isActionable: true, sortScore: 10, alternatives: bumpAlts };
   942→        }
   943→        if (opp.last_bump_date) {
   944→          const daysSince = Math.floor((Date.now() - new Date(opp.last_bump_date).getTime()) / (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24));
   945→          if (daysSince >= 3) {
   946→            return { label: `Bump #${opp.bump_number + 1}`, hint: `Last bump ${daysSince}d ago — send follow-up DM`, priority: 'high', actionType: 'bump', isActionable: true, sortScore: 11, alternatives: bumpAlts };
   947→          }
   948→          const daysLeft = 3 - daysSince;
   949→          return { label: `Wait ${daysLeft}d`, hint: 'Too soon to bump again', priority: 'wait', actionType: 'none', isActionable: false, sortScore: 100, alternatives: [] };
   950→        }
   951→        return { label: `Bump #${opp.bump_number + 1}`, hint: 'Send follow-up DM', priority: 'high', actionType: 'bump', isActionable: true, sortScore: 11, alternatives: bumpAlts };
   952→      }
   953→
   954→      // Fix #3: warm/interested — open detail to enter TG handle first, "Got TG!" is quick alt after
   955→      case 'warm':
   956→        if (opp.warm_sub_state === 'interested') {

   958→            { label: 'Got TG!', actionType: 'stage_change', targetStage: 'tg_intro', variant: 'default', quick: true },

   960→          ]};

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./sql/migrations/010_add_message_templates_and_learning.sql`

```text
     1→-- ============================================================================
     2→-- Message Templates & Learning System
     3→-- ============================================================================
     4→-- This migration creates tables for storing message templates and learning
     5→-- from user feedback to continuously improve AI-generated messages.
     6→-- ============================================================================
     7→
     8→-- Enable pgvector if not already enabled (for message embeddings)
     9→CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector;
    10→
    11→-- ============================================================================
    12→-- 1. Message Templates Table
    13→-- ============================================================================
    14→-- Stores reusable message templates with variables
    15→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS message_templates (
    16→  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    17→  name TEXT NOT NULL,
    18→  message_type TEXT NOT NULL, -- 'proposal', 'nda_request', 'kol_list_delivery', etc.
    19→  subject TEXT,
    20→  content TEXT NOT NULL,
    21→  variables JSONB DEFAULT '[]'::jsonb, -- Array of variable names like ["CLIENT_NAME", "PROJECT_NAME"]
    22→  usage_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    23→  last_used_at TIMESTAMP,
    24→  is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true,
    25→  created_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
    26→  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
    27→  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
    28→);
    29→
    30→-- Index for faster template lookups
    31→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_message_templates_type ON message_templates(message_type);
    32→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_message_templates_active ON message_templates(is_active);
    33→
    34→-- ============================================================================
    35→-- 2. Message Examples Table (Learning Database)
    36→-- ============================================================================
    37→-- Stores actual messages sent to clients (for learning and context)
    38→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS client_message_examples (
    39→  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    40→  user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id) NOT NULL,
    41→  client_id UUID REFERENCES clients(id),
    42→  campaign_id UUID REFERENCES campaigns(id),
    43→  message_type TEXT NOT NULL,
    44→  subject TEXT,
    45→  content TEXT NOT NULL,
    46→
    47→  -- Template tracking
    48→  template_id UUID REFERENCES message_templates(id),
    49→  was_ai_generated BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
    50→  original_ai_content TEXT, -- Store what AI originally generated
    51→
    52→  -- User feedback
    53→  user_rating INTEGER CHECK (user_rating >= 1 AND user_rating <= 5),
    54→  was_edited BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
    55→  edit_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    56→  was_sent BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
    57→
    58→  -- Metadata
    59→  context_data JSONB, -- Store campaign/client data at time of generation
    60→  generation_parameters JSONB, -- Store tool parameters used
    61→
    62→  -- Vector embedding for similarity search
    63→  embedding VECTOR(1536),
    64→
    65→  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
    66→  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
    67→);
    68→
    69→-- Indexes for performance
    70→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_client_message_examples_user ON client_message_examples(user_id);
    71→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_client_message_examples_client ON client_message_examples(client_id);
    72→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_client_message_examples_campaign ON client_message_examples(campaign_id);
    73→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_client_message_examples_type ON client_message_examples(message_type);
    74→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_client_message_examples_rating ON client_message_examples(user_rating DESC);
    75→
    76→-- Vector similarity search index
    77→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_client_message_examples_embedding
    78→  ON client_message_examples
    79→  USING ivfflat (embedding vector_cosine_ops)
    80→  WITH (lists = 100);
    81→
    82→-- ============================================================================
    83→-- 3. Message Feedback Table
    84→-- ============================================================================
    85→-- Tracks detailed feedback on AI-generated messages
    86→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ai_message_feedback (
    87→  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    88→  message_example_id UUID REFERENCES client_message_examples(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    89→
    90→  -- Feedback details
    91→  feedback_type TEXT, -- 'edit', 'rating', 'sent', 'discarded'
    92→  before_content TEXT,
    93→  after_content TEXT,
    94→  edit_summary TEXT, -- What changed (auto-generated)
    95→
    96→  -- Learning data
    97→  user_comments TEXT,
    98→  helpful_score INTEGER CHECK (helpful_score >= 1 AND helpful_score <= 5),
    99→
   100→  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
   101→);
   102→
   103→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ai_message_feedback_message ON ai_message_feedback(message_example_id);
   104→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ai_message_feedback_type ON ai_message_feedback(feedback_type);
   105→
   106→-- ============================================================================
   107→-- 4. Template Usage Analytics
   108→-- ============================================================================
   109→-- Track template performance over time
   110→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS template_usage_analytics (
   111→  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   112→  template_id UUID REFERENCES message_templates(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   113→  user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id),
   114→
   115→  -- Usage metrics
   116→  generated_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
   117→  sent_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
   118→  edited_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
   119→  average_rating DECIMAL(3,2),
   120→
   121→  -- Time periods
   122→  period_start DATE NOT NULL,
   123→  period_end DATE NOT NULL,
   124→
   125→  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
   126→  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
   127→
   128→  UNIQUE(template_id, period_start, period_end)
   129→);
   130→
   131→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_template_usage_analytics_template ON template_usage_analytics(template_id);
   132→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_template_usage_analytics_period ON template_usage_analytics(period_start, period_end);
   133→
   134→-- ============================================================================
   135→-- 5. Row Level Security (RLS)
   136→-- ============================================================================
   137→
   138→-- Enable RLS on all tables
   139→ALTER TABLE message_templates ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
   140→ALTER TABLE client_message_examples ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
   141→ALTER TABLE ai_message_feedback ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
   142→ALTER TABLE template_usage_analytics ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
   143→
   144→-- Message Templates Policies
   145→CREATE POLICY "Users can view all active templates"
   146→  ON message_templates FOR SELECT
   147→  USING (is_active = true);
   148→
   149→CREATE POLICY "Admins can manage templates"
   150→  ON message_templates FOR ALL
   151→  USING (
   152→    EXISTS (
   153→      SELECT 1 FROM users
   154→      WHERE users.id = auth.uid()
   155→      AND users.role = 'admin'
   156→    )
   157→  );
   158→
   159→-- Client Message Examples Policies
   160→CREATE POLICY "Users can view their own message examples"
   161→  ON client_message_examples FOR SELECT
   162→  USING (user_id = auth.uid());
   163→
   164→CREATE POLICY "Users can create their own message examples"
   165→  ON client_message_examples FOR INSERT
   166→  WITH CHECK (user_id = auth.uid());
   167→
   168→CREATE POLICY "Users can update their own message examples"
   169→  ON client_message_examples FOR UPDATE
   170→  USING (user_id = auth.uid());
   171→
   172→CREATE POLICY "Users can delete their own message examples"
   173→  ON client_message_examples FOR DELETE
   174→  USING (user_id = auth.uid());
   175→
   176→-- Admins can see all examples
   177→CREATE POLICY "Admins can view all message examples"
   178→  ON client_message_examples FOR SELECT
   179→  USING (
   180→    EXISTS (
   181→      SELECT 1 FROM users
   182→      WHERE users.id = auth.uid()
   183→      AND users.role = 'admin'
   184→    )
   185→  );
   186→
   187→-- AI Message Feedback Policies
   188→CREATE POLICY "Users can view feedback on their messages"
   189→  ON ai_message_feedback FOR SELECT
   190→  USING (
   191→    EXISTS (
   192→      SELECT 1 FROM client_message_examples
   193→      WHERE client_message_examples.id = ai_message_feedback.message_example_id
   194→      AND client_message_examples.user_id = auth.uid()
   195→    )
   196→  );
   197→
   198→CREATE POLICY "Users can create feedback on their messages"
   199→  ON ai_message_feedback FOR INSERT
   200→  WITH CHECK (
   201→    EXISTS (
   202→      SELECT 1 FROM client_message_examples
   203→      WHERE client_message_examples.id = ai_message_feedback.message_example_id
   204→      AND client_message_examples.user_id = auth.uid()
   205→    )
   206→  );
   207→
   208→-- Template Usage Analytics Policies
   209→CREATE POLICY "Users can view their own analytics"
   210→  ON template_usage_analytics FOR SELECT
   211→  USING (user_id = auth.uid() OR user_id IS NULL);
   212→
   213→CREATE POLICY "Admins can view all analytics"
   214→  ON template_usage_analytics FOR SELECT
   215→  USING (
   216→    EXISTS (
   217→      SELECT 1 FROM users
   218→      WHERE users.id = auth.uid()
   219→      AND users.role = 'admin'
   220→    )
   221→  );
   222→
   223→-- ============================================================================
   224→-- 6. Helper Functions
   225→-- ============================================================================
   226→
   227→-- Function to update template usage count
   228→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION increment_template_usage(template_uuid UUID)
   229→RETURNS void AS $$
   230→BEGIN
   231→  UPDATE message_templates
   232→  SET
   233→    usage_count = usage_count + 1,
   234→    last_used_at = NOW(),
   235→    updated_at = NOW()
   236→  WHERE id = template_uuid;
   237→END;
   238→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
   239→
   240→-- Function to calculate edit similarity (how much was changed)
   241→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION calculate_edit_similarity(
   242→  original_text TEXT,
   243→  edited_text TEXT
   244→)
   245→RETURNS DECIMAL AS $$
   246→DECLARE
   247→  original_length INTEGER;
   248→  edited_length INTEGER;
   249→  max_length INTEGER;
   250→BEGIN
   251→  original_length := LENGTH(original_text);
   252→  edited_length := LENGTH(edited_text);
   253→  max_length := GREATEST(original_length, edited_length);
   254→
   255→  IF max_length = 0 THEN
   256→    RETURN 1.0;
   257→  END IF;
   258→
   259→  -- Simple similarity based on length difference
   260→  -- More sophisticated would use Levenshtein distance
   261→  RETURN 1.0 - (ABS(original_length - edited_length)::DECIMAL / max_length);
   262→END;
   263→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
   264→
   265→-- ============================================================================
   266→-- 7. Insert Initial Templates
   267→-- ============================================================================

All posts will be updated in our tracker sheet [HERE].\n\nLet us know if you want a final sync today or need anything else ahead of go-time!',
   325→  '[]'::jsonb
   326→),
   327→(
   328→  'Final Campaign Report',
   329→  'final_report',
   330→  NULL,
   331→  E'Hey team,\n\nWe''re happy to share the campaign report for your [TGE_LAUNCH], including:\n- Key metrics\n- Performance highlights\n- Audience engagement\n- Post-campaign recommendations\n\nYou can view the report here: [CAMPAIGN_REPORT_LINK]\n\nLet us know if you have any questions or would like to schedule a time to discuss the findings in more detail.\n\nThanks again for the opportunity to collaborate — we''re excited about what''s next.',
   332→  '["TGE_LAUNCH", "CAMPAIGN_REPORT_LINK"]'::jsonb
   333→);
   334→
   335→-- ============================================================================
   336→-- 8. Vector Similarity Search Function
   337→-- ============================================================================
   338→
   339→-- Function to search for similar messages using vector embeddings
   340→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION search_similar_messages(
   341→  query_embedding TEXT,
   342→  message_type_filter TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
   343→  match_threshold FLOAT DEFAULT 0.7,
   344→  match_count INT DEFAULT 5
   345→)
   346→RETURNS TABLE (
   347→  id UUID,
   348→  user_id UUID,
   349→  message_type TEXT,
   350→  content TEXT,
   351→  user_rating INTEGER,
   352→  was_sent BOOLEAN,
   353→  similarity FLOAT
   354→) AS $$
   355→BEGIN
   356→  RETURN QUERY
   357→  SELECT
   358→    cme.id,
   359→    cme.user_id,
   360→    cme.message_type,
   361→    cme.content,
   362→    cme.user_rating,
   363→    cme.was_sent,
   364→    1 - (cme.embedding <=> query_embedding::vector) AS similarity
   365→  FROM client_message_examples cme
   366→  WHERE
   367→    (message_type_filter IS NULL OR cme.message_type = message_type_filter)
   368→    AND cme.was_sent = true -- Only learn from messages that were actually sent
   369→    AND 1 - (cme.embedding <=> query_embedding::vector) > match_threshold
   370→  ORDER BY cme.embedding <=> query_embedding::vector
   371→  LIMIT match_count;
   372→END;
   373→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
   374→
   375→-- ============================================================================
   376→-- 9. Create updated_at trigger
   377→-- ============================================================================
   378→
   379→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_updated_at_column()
   380→RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
   381→BEGIN
   382→  NEW.updated_at = NOW();
   383→  RETURN NEW;
   384→END;
   385→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
   386→
   387→CREATE TRIGGER update_message_templates_updated_at
   388→  BEFORE UPDATE ON message_templates
   389→  FOR EACH ROW
   390→  EXECUTE FUNCTION update_updated_at_column();
   391→
   392→CREATE TRIGGER update_client_message_examples_updated_at
   393→  BEFORE UPDATE ON client_message_examples
   394→  FOR EACH ROW
   395→  EXECUTE FUNCTION update_updated_at_column();
   396→
   397→CREATE TRIGGER update_template_usage_analytics_updated_at
   398→  BEFORE UPDATE ON template_usage_analytics
   399→  FOR EACH ROW
   400→  EXECUTE FUNCTION update_updated_at_column();
   401→
```
</details></tool-use>

   140	  dm_account?: DmAccount;

   142	  temperature_score?: number;

   157	  opportunity_id: string;
   158	  type: ActivityType;
