   164	}
   165	
   166	// ============================================
   167	// Sales Pipeline Service
   168	// ============================================
   169	
   170	export class SalesPipelineService {
   171	  // ----------------------------------------
   172	  // OPPORTUNITIES
   173	  // ----------------------------------------
   174	
   175	  static async getAll(): Promise<SalesPipelineOpportunity[]> {
   176	    const { data, error } = await supabase
   177	      .from('crm_opportunities')
   178	      .select(`
   179	        *,
   180	        affiliate:crm_affiliates(*),
   181	        client:clients!crm_opportunities_client_id_fkey(id, name)
   182	      `)
   183	      .in('stage', ALL_V2_STAGES)
   184	      .order('position', { ascending: true })
   185	      .order('created_at', { ascending: false });
   186	
   187	    if (error) {
   188	      console.error('Error fetching sales pipeline opportunities:', error);
   189	      throw error;
   190	    }
   191	    return (data || []) as SalesPipelineOpportunity[];
   192	  }
   193	
   194	  static async create(data: CreateSalesPipelineOpportunityData): Promise<SalesPipelineOpportunity> {
   195	    // Dedup check: name + tg_handle
   196	    if (data.name && data.tg_handle) {
   197	      const dedupKey = `${data.name.toLowerCase().trim()}::${data.tg_handle.toLowerCase().trim()}`;
   198	      const { data: existing } = await supabase
   199	        .from('crm_opportunities')
   200	        .select('id')
   201	        .eq('dedup_key', dedupKey)
   202	        .maybeSingle();
   203	
   204	      if (existing) {
   205	        throw new Error('A deal with this name and TG handle already exists');
   206	      }
   207	
   208	      (data as any).dedup_key = dedupKey;
   209	    }
   210	
   211	    const insertData = {
   212	      ...data,
   213	      stage: data.stage || 'cold_dm',
   214	    };
   215	
   216	    const { data: created, error } = await supabase
   217	      .from('crm_opportunities')
   218	      .insert([insertData])
   219	      .select()
   220	      .single();
   221	
   222	    if (error) throw error;
   223	
   224	    // Record stage history
   225	    await CRMService.recordStageHistory('opportunity', created.id, null, insertData.stage || 'cold_dm');
   226	
   227	    // Auto-calculate temperature score
   228	    await this.recalcTemperature(created.id);
   229	
   230	    return created as SalesPipelineOpportunity;
   231	  }
   232	
   233	  static async update(
   234	    id: string,
   235	    updates: Partial<CreateSalesPipelineOpportunityData> & {
   236	      warm_sub_state?: WarmSubState | null;
   237	      orbit_reason?: OrbitReason | null;
   238	      closed_lost_reason?: string | null;
   239	      next_meeting_at?: string | null;
   240	      next_meeting_type?: string | null;
   241	      bump_number?: number;
   242	      last_bump_date?: string | null;
   243	      bucket?: Bucket | null;
   244	      calendly_sent_via?: CalendlySentVia;
   245	      calendly_sent_date?: string | null;
   246	      calendly_booked_date?: string | null;
   247	      gc_opened?: string;
   248	    }
   249	  ): Promise<SalesPipelineOpportunity> {
   250	    // Get current state for stage history
   251	    const { data: current } = await supabase
   252	      .from('crm_opportunities')
   253	      .select('*')
   254	      .eq('id', id)
   255	      .single();
   256	
   257	    const updateData: any = { ...updates, updated_at: new Date().toISOString() };
   258	
   259	    // Auto-set timestamps on stage transitions
   260	    if (updates.stage && current) {
   261	      if (updates.stage === 'discovery_done' && !current.discovery_call_at) {
   262	        updateData.discovery_call_at = new Date().toISOString();
   263	      }
   264	      if (updates.stage === 'proposal_sent' && !current.proposal_sent_at) {
   265	        updateData.proposal_sent_at = new Date().toISOString();
   266	      }
   267	      if ((updates.stage === 'v2_closed_won' || updates.stage === 'v2_closed_lost') && !current.closed_at) {
   268	        updateData.closed_at = new Date().toISOString();
   269	      }
   270	    }
   271	
   272	    // Auto-set bucket_changed_at
   273	    if (updates.bucket && current && updates.bucket !== current.bucket) {
   274	      updateData.bucket_changed_at = new Date().toISOString();
   275	    }
   276	
   277	    const { data: updated, error } = await supabase
   278	      .from('crm_opportunities')
   279	      .update(updateData)
   280	      .eq('id', id)
   281	      .select()
   282	      .single();
   283	
   284	    if (error) throw error;
   285	
   286	    // Record stage change
   287	    if (updates.stage && current && current.stage !== updates.stage) {
   288	      await CRMService.recordStageHistory('opportunity', id, current.stage, updates.stage);
   289	    }
   290	
   291	    // Recalculate temperature score after stage/bucket/key field changes
   292	    await this.recalcTemperature(id);
   293	
   294	    return updated as SalesPipelineOpportunity;
   295	  }
   296	
   297	  static async delete(id: string): Promise<void> {
   298	    const { error } = await supabase
   299	      .from('crm_opportunities')
   300	      .delete()
```

   408	  static async getBAMFAMViolations(): Promise<SalesPipelineOpportunity[]> {
   409	    // BAMFAM: past discovery_done with no next_meeting or overdue next_meeting
   410	    const postDiscoveryStages: SalesPipelineStage[] = [
   411	      'discovery_done', 'proposal_sent', 'proposal_call', 'v2_contract',
   412	    ];
   413	
   414	    const { data, error } = await supabase
   415	      .from('crm_opportunities')
   416	      .select(`
   417	        *,
   418	        affiliate:crm_affiliates(*),
   419	        client:clients!crm_opportunities_client_id_fkey(id, name)
   420	      `)
   421	      .in('stage', postDiscoveryStages)
   422	      .or('next_meeting_at.is.null,next_meeting_at.lt.' + new Date().toISOString());
   423	
   424	    if (error) throw error;
   425	    return (data || []) as SalesPipelineOpportunity[];
   426	  }

   428	  // ----------------------------------------
   429	  // STAGES HELPER
   430	  // ----------------------------------------
   431	
   432	  static getAvailableStages(dmAccount: DmAccount): SalesPipelineStage[] {
   433	    if (dmAccount === 'closer') return PATH_A_STAGES;
   434	    return PATH_B_STAGES;
   435	  }
   436	
   437	  // ----------------------------------------
   438	  // METRICS
   439	  // ----------------------------------------
   440	
   441	  static async getMetrics(): Promise<{
   442	    totalCount: number;
   443	    bucketA: number;
   444	    bucketB: number;
   445	    bucketC: number;
   446	    activeValue: number;
   447	    bamfamViolations: number;
   448	  }> {
   449	    const { data, error } = await supabase
   450	      .from('crm_opportunities')
   451	      .select('stage, bucket, deal_value, next_meeting_at')
   452	      .in('stage', ALL_V2_STAGES);
   453	
   454	    if (error) throw error;
   455	
   456	    const opps = data || [];
   457	    const activeStages = PIPELINE_STAGES;
   458	    const active = opps.filter(o => activeStages.includes(o.stage as SalesPipelineStage));
   459	
   460	    const postDiscovery = ['discovery_done', 'proposal_sent', 'proposal_call', 'v2_contract'];
   461	    const now = new Date().toISOString();
   462	    const bamfam = opps.filter(o =>
   463	      postDiscovery.includes(o.stage) &&
   464	      (!o.next_meeting_at || o.next_meeting_at < now)
   465	    );
   466	
   467	    return {
   468	      totalCount: opps.length,
   469	      bucketA: opps.filter(o => o.bucket === 'A').length,
   470	      bucketB: opps.filter(o => o.bucket === 'B').length,
   471	      bucketC: opps.filter(o => o.bucket === 'C').length,
   472	      activeValue: active.reduce((sum, o) => sum + (o.deal_value || 0), 0),
   473	      bamfamViolations: bamfam.length,
   474	    };
   475	  }
   476	
   477	  // ----------------------------------------
   478	  // POSITION UPDATES
   479	  // ----------------------------------------
   480	
   481	  static async updatePositions(positions: { id: string; position: number; stage?: OpportunityStage }[]): Promise<void> {
   482	    const updates = positions.map(({ id, position, stage }) => {
   483	      const updateData: any = { position, updated_at: new Date().toISOString() };
   484	      if (stage) updateData.stage = stage;
   485	      return supabase
   486	        .from('crm_opportunities')
   487	        .update(updateData)
   488	        .eq('id', id);
   489	    });
   490	
   491	    const results = await Promise.all(updates);
   492	    const errors = results.filter(r => r.error);
   493	    if (errors.length > 0) {
   494	      console.error('Errors updating positions:', errors);
   495	      throw errors[0].error;
   496	    }
   497	  }
   498

```text
   500	  // COLD DM PAGINATED (Outreach tab)
   501	  // ----------------------------------------
   502	
   503	  static async getColdDmsPaginated(
   504	    page: number,
   505	    pageSize: number,
   506	    filters: {
   507	      dm_account?: DmAccount;
   508	      bucket?: Bucket;
   509	      bumpRange?: 'none' | '1-2' | '3+';
   510	      searchTerm?: string;
   511	      sortBy?: string;
   512	      sortAsc?: boolean;
   513	      owner_id?: string;
   514	    }
   515	  ): Promise<{ data: SalesPipelineOpportunity[]; count: number }> {
   516	    const from = (page - 1) * pageSize;
   517	    const to = from + pageSize - 1;
   518	
   519	    let query = supabase
   520	      .from('crm_opportunities')
   521	      .select(`
   522	        *,
   523	        affiliate:crm_affiliates(*),
   524	        client:clients!crm_opportunities_client_id_fkey(id, name)
   525	      `, { count: 'exact' })
   526	      .eq('stage', 'cold_dm');
   527	
   528	    // Optional filters
   529	    if (filters.dm_account) {
   530	      query = query.eq('dm_account', filters.dm_account);
   531	    }
   532	    if (filters.bucket) {
   533	      query = query.eq('bucket', filters.bucket);
   534	    }
   535	    if (filters.searchTerm) {
   536	      query = query.ilike('name', `%${filters.searchTerm}%`);
   537	    }
   538	    if (filters.owner_id) {
   539	      query = query.eq('owner_id', filters.owner_id);
   540	    }
   541	    if (filters.bumpRange === 'none') {
   542	      query = query.eq('bump_number', 0);
   543	    } else if (filters.bumpRange === '1-2') {
   544	      query = query.gte('bump_number', 1).lte('bump_number', 2);
   545	    } else if (filters.bumpRange === '3+') {
   546	      query = query.gte('bump_number', 3);
   547	    }
   548	
   549	    // Sorting
   550	    const sortField = filters.sortBy || 'created_at';
   551	    const ascending = filters.sortAsc ?? false;
   552	    query = query.order(sortField, { ascending });
   553	
   554	    // Pagination
   555	    query = query.range(from, to);
   556	
   557	    const { data, error, count } = await query;
   558	
   559	    if (error) {
   560	      console.error('Error fetching cold DMs paginated:', error);
   561	      throw error;
   562	    }
   563	
   564	    return { data: (data || []) as SalesPipelineOpportunity[], count: count || 0 };
   565	  }
   566	
   567	  // ----------------------------------------
   568	  // BULK OPERATIONS
   569	  // ----------------------------------------
   570	
   571	  // ----------------------------------------
   572	  // TEMPERATURE SCORE
   573	  // ----------------------------------------
   574	
   575	  static async recalcTemperature(id: string): Promise<number | null> {
   576	    const { data, error } = await supabase.rpc('calculate_temperature_score', { opp_id: id });
   577	    if (error) {
   578	      console.error('Error recalculating temperature:', error);
   579	      return null;
   580	    }
   581	    return data;
   582	  }
   583	
   584	  static async recalcAllTemperatures(): Promise<number | null> {
   585	    const { data, error } = await supabase.rpc('recalculate_all_temperature_scores');
   586	    if (error) {
   587	      console.error('Error recalculating all temperatures:', error);
   588	      return null;
   589	    }
   590	    return data;
   591	  }
   592	
   593	  // ----------------------------------------
   594	  // BULK OPERATIONS
   595	  // ----------------------------------------
   596	
   597	  static async bulkRecordBump(ids: string[]): Promise<void> {
   598	    await Promise.all(ids.map(id => this.recordBump(id)));
   599	  }
   600	
   601	  static async bulkUpdateStage(ids: string[], stage: SalesPipelineStage): Promise<void> {
   602	    const now = new Date().toISOString();
   603	    const updates = ids.map(id =>
   604	      supabase
   605	        .from('crm_opportunities')
   606	        .update({ stage, updated_at: now })
   607	        .eq('id', id)
   608	    );
   609	
   610	    const results = await Promise.all(updates);
   611	    const errors = results.filter(r => r.error);
   612	    if (errors.length > 0) {
   613	      console.error('Errors in bulk stage update:', errors);
   614	      throw errors[0].error;
   615	    }
   616	  }
   617	
   618	  static async bulkDelete(ids: string[]): Promise<void> {
   619	    const deletes = ids.map(id =>
   620	      supabase
   621	        .from('crm_opportunities')
   622	        .delete()
   623	        .eq('id', id)
   624	    );
   625	
   626	    const results = await Promise.all(deletes);
   627	    const errors = results.filter(r => r.error);
   628	    if (errors.length > 0) {
   629	      console.error('Errors in bulk delete:', errors);
   630	      throw errors[0].error;
   631	    }
   632	  }
   633	}

I now have everything needed. Here is a complete breakdown of all four areas.

---

## 1. `getNextAction` — Stages and Bump Labels

**File:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/crm/sales-pipeline/page.tsx` (lines 854–1055)

The function returns an object with `{ label, hint, priority, actionType, targetStage, isActionable, sortScore, alternatives }`.

### Return types used

```ts
type ActionPriority = 'urgent' | 'high' | 'medium' | 'low' | 'wait';
type ActionExecutionType = 'bump' | 'stage_change' | 'open_detail' | 'none';
```

### Stage-by-stage labels

| Stage | Condition | Primary Label | actionType |
|---|---|---|---|
| (any active, past meeting) | `hoursSinceMeeting` 0–168, not yet logged | `"Log Meeting Outcome"` | `open_detail` |
| (post-discovery) | no future meeting, no proposal yet | `"Send Proposal"` | `stage_change → proposal_sent` |
| (post-discovery) | no future meeting | `"Book Next Meeting!"` | `open_detail` |
| `v2_contract` | always | `"Chase Signature"` | `open_detail` |
| `proposal_call` | always | `"To Contract"` | `stage_change → v2_contract` |
| **`cold_dm`** | `bump_number >= 4` | `"Review → Orbit"` | `stage_change → orbit` |
| **`cold_dm`** | `bump_number === 0 && !last_bump_date` | **`"Send First DM"`** | `bump` |
| **`cold_dm`** | `last_bump_date` exists, `daysSince >= 3` | **`"Bump #${opp.bump_number + 1}"`** | `bump` |
| **`cold_dm`** | `last_bump_date` exists, `daysSince < 3` | `"Wait ${daysLeft}d"` | `none` |
| **`cold_dm`** | fallback (no last_bump_date, bump_number > 0) | **`"Bump #${opp.bump_number + 1}"`** | `bump` |
| `warm` | `warm_sub_state === 'interested'` | `"Get TG Handle"` | `open_detail` |
| `warm` | otherwise | `"Follow Up"` | `open_detail` |
| `tg_intro` | `daysSinceContact >= 7` | `"Re-engage or Orbit"` | `open_detail` |
| `tg_intro` | otherwise | `"Booked!"` | `stage_change → booked` |
| `booked` | no `next_meeting_at` | `"Schedule Meeting"` | `open_detail` |
| `booked` | meeting in future | `"Prep for Meeting"` | `open_detail` |
| `booked` | meeting in past | `"Discovery Done"` | `stage_change → discovery_done` |
| `discovery_done` | no `proposal_sent_at` | `"Send Proposal"` | `stage_change → proposal_sent` |
| `discovery_done` | proposal already sent | `"Update Stage"` | `stage_change → proposal_sent` |
| `proposal_sent` | always | `"Follow Up Proposal"` | `open_detail` |
| `orbit` | `daysSinceUpdated > 90` | `"Resurrect"` | `open_detail` |
| `orbit` | otherwise | `"In Orbit"` | `none` |
| `nurture` | `daysSinceNurtureContact >= 30` | `"Check In"` | `open_detail` |

**Key bump label pattern:** `"Send First DM"` is only shown when `bump_number === 0 && !last_bump_date`. Every subsequent bump shows `"Bump #${opp.bump_number + 1}"` (i.e., "Bump #1", "Bump #2", "Bump #3" until `bump_number >= 4` triggers orbit).

---

## 2. `bump_count` / `bump_number` Usage

There is no field named `bump_count` anywhere in this codebase. The field is called **`bump_number`** throughout.

**On the `SalesPipelineOpportunity` interface** (line 104):
```ts
bump_number: number;
last_bump_date: string | null;
```

**In `getNextAction`** (page.tsx):
- `opp.bump_number >= 4` → triggers "Review → Orbit"
- `opp.bump_number === 0 && !opp.last_bump_date` → "Send First DM"
- `Bump #${opp.bump_number + 1}` → shows the next bump number to send

**In `getColdDmsPaginated` filters** (service, line 541–547):
```ts
if (filters.bumpRange === 'none')      query = query.eq('bump_number', 0);
else if (filters.bumpRange === '1-2')  query = query.gte('bump_number', 1).lte('bump_number', 2);
else if (filters.bumpRange === '3+')   query = query.gte('bump_number', 3);
```

---

## 3. `SalesPipelineService` — All Methods and Tables

**File:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/salesPipelineService.ts`

### Supabase Tables Referenced

| Table | Operations |
|---|---|
| `crm_opportunities` | SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE (primary table for all ops) |
| `crm_affiliates` | JOIN via `affiliate:crm_affiliates(*)` |
| `clients` | JOIN via `client:clients!crm_opportunities_client_id_fkey(id, name)` |
| `crm_activities` | SELECT, INSERT |

### RPC Functions Called

| RPC | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `calculate_temperature_score(opp_id)` | Recalculate temperature for one opp |
| `recalculate_all_temperature_scores()` | Recalculate all temperatures |

### All Public Static Methods

| Method | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `getAll()` | `→ SalesPipelineOpportunity[]` | Fetch all opps in `ALL_V2_STAGES`, ordered by position then created_at |
| `create(data)` | `CreateSalesPipelineOpportunityData → SalesPipelineOpportunity` | Insert new opp, dedup check on name+tg_handle, records stage history, recalcs temperature |
| `update(id, updates)` | `Partial<...> → SalesPipelineOpportunity` | Update opp, auto-sets timestamps on stage transitions, records stage history, recalcs temperature |
| `delete(id)` | `→ void` | Hard delete from `crm_opportunities` |
| `recordBump(id)` | `→ SalesPipelineOpportunity` | Increments `bump_number`, sets `last_bump_date` + `last_contacted_at`, creates a `bump` activity |
| `reduceBump(id)` | `→ SalesPipelineOpportunity` | Decrements `bump_number` (floor 0), no date changes |
| `getActivities(opportunityId)` | `→ CRMActivity[]` | Fetch all activities for an opp, desc by created_at |
| `createActivity(data)` | `CreateActivityData → CRMActivity` | Insert into `crm_activities`, recalcs temperature |
| `getBAMFAMViolations()` | `→ SalesPipelineOpportunity[]` | Post-discovery opps with null or past `next_meeting_at` |
| `getAvailableStages(dmAccount)` | `→ SalesPipelineStage[]` | Returns PATH_A (closer) or PATH_B (sdr/other) stages |
| `getMetrics()` | `→ { totalCount, bucketA, bucketB, bucketC, activeValue, bamfamViolations }` | Aggregate counts from `crm_opportunities` |
| `updatePositions(positions)` | `→ void` | Bulk update `position` (and optionally `stage`) for drag-and-drop |
| `getColdDmsPaginated(page, pageSize, filters)` | `→ { data, count }` | Paginated cold_dm query with dm_account, bucket, bumpRange, search, sort filters |
| `recalcTemperature(id)` | `→ number | null` | Call RPC for one opp |
| `recalcAllTemperatures()` | `→ number | null` | Call RPC for all opps |
| `bulkRecordBump(ids)` | `→ void` | Parallel `recordBump` across multiple ids |
| `bulkUpdateStage(ids, stage)` | `→ void` | Parallel stage update for multiple ids |
| `bulkDelete(ids)` | `→ void` | Parallel delete for multiple ids |

---

## 4. `message_templates` Table Schema

**File:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/sql/migrations/010_add_message_templates_and_learning.sql`

```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS message_templates (
  id           UUID      PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  name         TEXT      NOT NULL,
  message_type TEXT      NOT NULL,  -- e.g. 'proposal', 'nda_request', 'kol_list_delivery'
  subject      TEXT,                -- nullable (used for email subject lines)
  content      TEXT      NOT NULL,
  variables    JSONB     DEFAULT '[]'::jsonb,  -- e.g. ["CLIENT_NAME", "PROJECT_NAME"]
  usage_count  INTEGER   DEFAULT 0,
  last_used_at TIMESTAMP,
  is_active    BOOLEAN   DEFAULT true,
  created_by   UUID      REFERENCES users(id),
  created_at   TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at   TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);
```

Indexes: `idx_message_templates_type` on `message_type`, `idx_message_templates_active` on `is_active`.

RLS: All authenticated users can SELECT where `is_active = true`; only `admin` role users can INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE.

### Seeded `message_type` values (from initial INSERT block)

| `message_type` | `name` | Variables |
|---|---|---|
| `initial_outreach` | Initial Outreach / Proposal | `CLIENT_NAME`, `PROJECT_NAME`, `TGE_LAUNCH` |
| `nda_request` | NDA Request | (none) |
| `kol_list_access` | KOL List Access Coordination | (none) |
| `kol_list_delivery` | KOL List Delivery | `CLIENT_HANDLE`, `EMAIL_ADDRESS` |
| `final_kol_picks` | Final KOL Picks & Strategy | `CLIENT_HANDLE` |
| `post_call_followup` | Post-Call Follow-Up | `CLIENT_HANDLE` |
| `contract_activation` | Contract & Activation Details | `CLIENT_HANDLE`, `EMAIL_ADDRESS` |
| `activation_day` | Activation Day Update | (none) |
| `final_report` | Final Campaign Report | `TGE_LAUNCH`, `CAMPAIGN_REPORT_LINK` |

The migration also creates three companion tables: `client_message_examples` (stores actual sent messages with vector embeddings for AI learning), `ai_message_feedback` (detailed edit/rating feedback), and `template_usage_analytics` (aggregated performance by time period).

```text
     1→# Activity Log Popup for Action Execution
     2→
     3→## Context
     4→
     5→Currently, when users execute actions from the Actions tab (bumps, stage changes, etc.), no activity is logged. The activity timeline in the slide-over is completely manual. We need to:
     6→1. Show an **activity log popup** when actions are executed, pre-filled with the right activity type/title, so users can add context before it's saved
     7→2. Make the **"Book Next Meeting"** action show a popup with a meeting date/time picker (linked to `next_meeting_at`) + activity form
     8→3. Show a **time picker** in the activity form when the type is "meeting"
     9→
    10→## What Changes
    11→
    12→**Single file**: `app/crm/sales-pipeline/page.tsx`
    13→
    14→### 1. New state: `activityLogPrompt`
    15→
    16→```typescript
    17→const [activityLogPrompt, setActivityLogPrompt] = useState<{
    18→  oppId: string;
    19→  oppName: string;
    20→  type: ActivityType;
    21→  title: string;
    22→  showMeetingPicker?: boolean; // true for "Book Next Meeting" actions
    23→} | null>(null);
    24→```
    25→
    26→### 2. New state: `activityLogForm`
    27→
    28→Separate from the existing `activityForm` (which lives in the slide-over). This form is for the popup dialog:
    29→
    30→```typescript
    31→const [activityLogForm, setActivityLogForm] = useState<{
    32→  description: string;
    33→  outcome: string;
    34→  next_step: string;
    35→  next_step_date?: string;
    36→  meeting_date?: string;  // only for meeting type — links to next_meeting_at
    37→  meeting_time?: string;  // hour/minute for the meeting
    38→}>({ description: '', outcome: '', next_step: '' });
    39→```
    40→
    41→### 3. Modify `handleActionExecute()`
    42→
    43→After executing bump/stage_change, instead of just finishing, open the activity log popup with pre-filled data:
    44→
    45→| Action | Activity Type | Pre-filled Title | showMeetingPicker |
    46→|---|---|---|---|
    47→| `bump` (Send First DM, Bump #N) | `message` | "Bump #N sent" | no |
    48→| stage_change to `warm` ("Replied!") | `message` | "KOL replied" | no |
    49→| stage_change to `tg_intro` ("Got TG!") | `message` | "Got TG handle" | no |
    50→| stage_change to `booked` ("Booked!") | `meeting` | "Meeting booked" | yes |
    51→| stage_change to `discovery_done` | `meeting` | "Discovery call completed" | no |
    52→| stage_change to `proposal_sent` | `proposal` | "Proposal sent" | no |
    53→| stage_change to `v2_contract` ("To Contract") | `call` | "Moving to contract" | no |
    54→| stage_change to `v2_closed_won` ("Signed!") | `note` | "Deal closed won!" | no |
    55→| `open_detail` for "Book Next Meeting!" | `meeting` | "Meeting scheduled" | yes |
    56→| `open_detail` for "Schedule Meeting" | `meeting` | "Meeting scheduled" | yes |
    57→| Other `open_detail` actions | no popup, open slide-over as before | — | — |
    58→
    59→For "Book Next Meeting!" and "Schedule Meeting": instead of opening the slide-over, show the activity log popup with `showMeetingPicker: true`. The meeting date/time from the popup will update `next_meeting_at` on the opportunity.
    60→
    61→### 4. `confirmActivityLog()` handler
    62→
    63→When user submits the popup:
    64→1. Create the activity via `SalesPipelineService.createActivity()`
    65→2. If `showMeetingPicker` and a meeting date was set → also call `SalesPipelineService.update(oppId, { next_meeting_at })` to link the meeting date
    66→3. Reset form and close popup
    67→4. Refetch data
    68→
    69→### 5. `renderActivityLogPrompt()` — Dialog UI
    70→
    71→A `<Dialog>` (same pattern as orbitPrompt/closedLostPrompt) with `z-[80]`:
    72→
    73→- **Header**: "Log Activity — {oppName}"
    74→- **Pre-filled type badge** (read-only, showing the activity type icon + label)
    75→- **Pre-filled title** (editable Input)
    76→- If `showMeetingPicker`:
    77→  - **Meeting Date** — Popover + CalendarPicker (same style as edit form, z-[90])
    78→  - **Meeting Time** — Popover + hour/minute scroll columns (same style as edit form, z-[90])
    79→- **Description** (Textarea, optional)
    80→- **Outcome** (Input, optional)
    81→- **Next Step** (Input, optional)
    82→- **Next Step Date** (Popover + CalendarPicker, optional)
    83→- **Footer**: Cancel + "Log Activity" button
    84→
    85→### 6. Activity form in slide-over — show time picker for "meeting" type
    86→
    87→In the existing activity form (slide-over, ~line 2670), when `activityForm.type === 'meeting'`:
    88→- Show an additional **Meeting Date** (Popover + CalendarPicker) and **Meeting Time** (Popover + hour/minute columns) row
    89→- On submit, if meeting date is set, also update `next_meeting_at` on the opportunity
    90→
    91→### 7. Render the dialog
    92→
    93→Add `{renderActivityLogPrompt()}` alongside `{renderOrbitPrompt()}` and `{renderClosedLostPrompt()}` at the bottom of the component return.
    94→
    95→## Key Design Decisions
    96→
    97→- **Popup appears AFTER the action executes** (for bumps/stage changes) — the action already happened, this is to log context. The popup is skippable (Cancel still saves the action, just without an activity log).
    98→- **"Book Next Meeting" is special** — the popup appears INSTEAD of the slide-over, with a meeting date picker that writes to `next_meeting_at`.
    99→- **Reuses existing `SalesPipelineService.createActivity()`** — no service changes needed.
   100→- **Separate form state** from slide-over's `activityForm` to avoid interference.
   101→
   102→## Verification
   103→
   104→1. `npm run build` passes
   105→2. Bump action → popup appears with type "message", title "Bump #N sent", user can add description
   106→3. Stage change → popup appears with appropriate type/title
   107→4. "Book Next Meeting!" → popup shows meeting date/time pickers + activity form
   108→5. Meeting date from popup updates `next_meeting_at` on the opportunity
   109→6. Cancel on popup still preserves the executed action (bump/stage change already happened)
   110→7. In slide-over activity form, selecting "meeting" type shows date/time pickers
   111→8. Submitting meeting activity from slide-over also updates `next_meeting_at`
   112→
```

```md
# Overall Tab Count, Hide Pipeline, Sales Message Templates

## Context

Three changes requested:
1. Show total opportunity count badge on the "Overall" tab
2. Hide the old "Pipeline" link from the sidebar (only keep "Sales")
3. Add stage-specific DM/message templates + bump templates within the sales pipeline page — separate from the client templates system

## Changes

### 1. Overall Tab — Show Count Badge

**File:** `app/crm/sales-pipeline/page.tsx`

Add a badge to the Overview `TabsTrigger` showing total non-terminal opportunities:
\```tsx
<TabsTrigger value="overview">
  <LayoutGrid /> Overall
  <Badge>{opportunities.filter(o => !['v2_closed_won','v2_closed_lost'].includes(o.stage)).length}</Badge>
</TabsTrigger>
\```

### 2. Hide Pipeline from Sidebar

**File:** `components/Sidebar.tsx` (~lines 282-296)

Remove the entire `{/* Pipeline tab */}` Link block (`/crm/pipeline`). Keep the Sales Pipeline link (`/crm/sales-pipeline`).

### 3. Sales Message Templates

This is a stage-specific template system for outreach DMs and bumps — completely separate from the existing `message_templates` table (which is for client communication).

#### 3a. DB Migration — `sales_dm_templates` table

\```sql
CREATE TABLE sales_dm_templates (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  name TEXT NOT NULL,
  stage TEXT NOT NULL,           -- 'cold_dm', 'warm', 'tg_intro', etc. OR 'bump'
  sub_type TEXT DEFAULT 'general', -- 'initial', 'bump_1', 'bump_2', 'bump_3', 'follow_up', 'general'
  content TEXT NOT NULL,
  variables JSONB DEFAULT '[]',  -- e.g. ["KOL_NAME", "PROJECT_NAME"]
  is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true,
  created_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);
\```

Stage values: `cold_dm`, `warm`, `tg_intro`, `booked`, `discovery_done`, `proposal_sent`, `proposal_call`, `v2_contract`, `bump`
Sub-type for bumps: `bump_1`, `bump_2`, `bump_3`

RLS: All authenticated users can SELECT; admin can INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE.

#### 3b. Service methods — `lib/salesPipelineService.ts`

Add to `SalesPipelineService`:
- `getTemplates()` → fetch all active templates ordered by stage, sub_type
- `createTemplate(data)` → insert
- `updateTemplate(id, data)` → update
- `deleteTemplate(id)` → delete

Add interface `SalesDmTemplate` matching the table.

#### 3c. UI — Templates tab in sales pipeline page

**File:** `app/crm/sales-pipeline/page.tsx`

Add a new top-level tab "Templates" (with `FileText` icon) after "Overall" in the TabsList.

The templates tab shows:
- **Stage filter** — horizontal pills to filter by stage (All / Cold DM / Warm / TG Intro / Bumps / etc.)
- **Template cards** — grid layout, each card shows:
  - Name, stage badge, sub-type badge
  - Content preview (truncated)
  - Copy button (copies content to clipboard)
  - Edit / Delete buttons
- **"+ New Template" button** — opens a dialog with:
  - Name (Input)
  - Stage (Select from stages + "bump")
  - Sub-type (Select — changes based on stage: for bump → bump_1/bump_2/bump_3, for others → general/initial/follow_up)
  - Content (Textarea with placeholder hint about `[KOL_NAME]` etc.)
- **Edit dialog** — same as create, pre-filled

Seed a few default templates on first load (or via migration):
- Cold DM: Initial outreach template
- Bump 1/2/3: Escalating follow-up templates
- Warm: Follow-up after reply
- TG Intro: First TG message

## Files Modified

1. `app/crm/sales-pipeline/page.tsx` — Overall badge + Templates tab UI
2. `components/Sidebar.tsx` — Remove Pipeline link
3. `lib/salesPipelineService.ts` — Template CRUD methods + interface
4. DB migration via Supabase MCP — `sales_dm_templates` table + seed data

## Verification

1. `npm run build` passes
2. Overall tab shows count of all active opportunities
3. Pipeline link gone from sidebar, Sales link still works
4. Templates tab shows stage-filtered template cards
5. Can create/edit/delete/copy templates
6. Bump templates filterable separately

```
